Preliminaries
Tempest / AL

Emma Dunn / NC-HH
David Hallam
Steve Moore
N. Bettesworth

Wed 24 June AM 1

24 Jun 202608:08

We hope to report today on the employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest & PQ vs DEFRA and the Rural Payments Agency.

24 Jun 202608:10

It is the first day of hearings so there may well applications or other business for the Tribunal to deal with. Official start time is 10 am.

24 Jun 202608:13

PQ & Tempest (the claimants or Cs) are claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

24 Jun 202608:15

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

24 Jun 202608:31

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

24 Jun 202608:35

tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/introduction…

24 Jun 202608:42

We've just been told by the clerk that new details will be provided within 'the next 20 minutes or so' for remote access.

24 Jun 202608:53

Abbreviations

PQ - anonymised claimant

ST - Samantha Tempest

DEFRA/R1 - Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, 1st respondent

RPA/R2 - Rural Payments Agency, 2nd respondent

Rs - 1st & 2nd respondents

SEEN/IP - Sex Equality and Equity Network - Civil Service

24 Jun 202608:56

EW - Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC - Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as intervenor

J - Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 - Panel member 1

P2 - Panel member 2

AL - Alex Line, barrister for Rs

JRL - JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

24 Jun 202609:04

HH - Helen Hogben, barrister for C

GC - gender critical or sex realist views

GI - gender identity

TW - transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender reassignment

TM - transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender reassignment

24 Jun 202609:10

AB - Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant.

24 Jun 202609:11

Catching up here: PQ has settled and is no longer part of the case.

Revised description: Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment.

24 Jun 202609:13

We have received new remote access details and are WFTCHTJ - waiting for the conference host to join.

Will return when we have any news on start time.

24 Jun 202609:34

Now 10:30, no update on start time for public hearing.

24 Jun 202609:52

We begin.

J - introduces himself and panel members. We have Ms Hogben, for C, Mr Line for gov, Ms Cunningham - for the intervenor. A number of preliminary matters that we need to address. Helpful short bundle of documents. What order should we address. If we can sort out the

24 Jun 202609:53

pronouns question first. J - I still make hand written notes.

The 1st q is to determine what name and ps (pronouns) for the C. The C's position is clear, what she wishes to be called. AL, you disagree with that. On the basis that we know what the C & R position is, I will

24 Jun 202609:54

ask NC,

AL/HH - both reserve opportunity to comment on any remarks by NC

NC - in a sense it's not for me to speak to first, C has asked the ET for direction, C wants wrong sex pronouns or no pronouns from me,

J - HH has made that clear

NC - I'm simply making the point that this

24 Jun 202609:56

is C's application, asking the ET to do something unusual and extraordinary, seeking to control intervenor's speech to use wrong sex pronouns or efforts to avoid pronouns, it's not a trivial matter. Intervenor will not use wrong sex pronouns for the C, because Intervenor does not

24 Jun 202609:58

believe that C is a woman.

J - even if order by the tribunal

NC - no, the request is for us to choose between wrong sex pronouns and only use his name. Of course, you could go further and order us to use wrong sex pronouns. If we have to do that it will affect our every

24 Jun 202609:59

utterance, and significantly impact the amount of effort required. Witnesses are asked to tell the truth, and advocates need to be able to advocate for our clients. I says it's wholly unjust to be asked to jump through these linguistic hoops. It is a cognitive effort to use

24 Jun 202610:00

wrong sex pronouns, requires a mental effort to think of the individual as the opposite sex. You do that by trying to think of the C as a woman, cognitively it's a much lighter load to try and think of C as woman, then to remember that the usual rules of grammar don't apply

24 Jun 202610:02

J - you find yourself in a difficult position, can we be brief on this

NC - you shouldn't interfere with the Intervenor and her advocates every utterance about the C, it's particularly important in this case because a witness and my main instructing party is autistic, its

24 Jun 202610:03

is particularly difficult for her to do something that requires untruth. The C says this is a matter of hearing management but it's a key part of the case itself. We have been taught and indeed the Tribunal has been taught that it is abusive to refer to trans people by correct

24 Jun 202610:04

sex pronouns. It is simply not. I also draw attention to the asymmetry here, we have not attempted to control the language of the C or advocate in any way and we would not think of interfering. We take a neutral position that all parties should use the language they see fit.

24 Jun 202610:06

We say that its a part of HH advocacy to constantly use feminine pronouns and language about ST. If you repeat a lie often enough people come to believe it. For example TWAW

J - that's not what we're here to discuss, can we be brief

NC - that's all I have to say about HH

24 Jun 202610:07

advocacy. But the C has asked that a key witness, who is autistic, who has sworn to tell the truth. This application to control the language of my witness is flatly contradictory to the Equal Treatment Bench Book which says witnesses should not be compelled to use wrong pronouns

24 Jun 202610:09

and it says nothing about the language of advocates because they would be an extraordinary violation of rights. This application is an example of the type of behaviour that gives rise to the tribunal - a draconian attempt to control language and discussion.

24 Jun 202610:10

It is a wholly unwarranted interference with Art 10 rights. This is a case in which it is plainly inappropriate for the Tribunal itself to use preferred pronouns. The C's case is that any manifestation of GC belief is harassment in the workplace. It is an important part of this

24 Jun 202610:11

story that he is male and the network that he is attempting to fit this scold's bridle to is primarily female. We say it is important that the C is male and that SEEN is largely female. The tribunal is a bullying act of a man seeking to silence women. You may feel inclined to

24 Jun 202610:12

use feminine pronouns out of kindness and courtesy. And those are good things in general. But by using those pronouns you are inherently accepting that it is unkind to not use them. I'm asking you to manifest impartiality between the parties and I'd like you to use name or C

24 Jun 202610:14

for C and avoid using gendered language.

HH - you have the letter from my instructing solicitors, sets out in detail our application. I am surprised by the intervenor's characterisation of application as extraordinary - follows to the the letter the ETBB (Equal Treatment Bench

24 Jun 202610:15

Book). It's about effective communication, it underlies this entire process, where the C is coming from, language is crucial because the deliberate use of he/him pronouns would have a profound effect on C's ability to give best evidence. Drawing Tribunals attention to

24 Jun 202610:16

C's previous experience on use of pronouns in earlier hearing. I didn't attend but C was x-examined at length using he/him pronouns and was traumatised by the experience. Because of that the judge in that case had intended to address C by preferred pronouns, the judge used he

24 Jun 202610:17

/him pronouns, accidental but had the effect of distressing C. This is a case that follows to the letter the guidance set out in the ETBB, could have tried to insist on everyone using she/her but acknowledges can't compel witnesses so is happy with no pronouns. This is a case of

24 Jun 202610:19

This is not a case of a rape victim being forced to use she/her pronouns, it is accepted that C has a GRC and is a TW. I'm grateful to the Rs for their approach to use preferred pronouns. We ask the Tribunal to do the same and witnesses to use neutral or no pronouns.

24 Jun 202610:22

J - we shall retire..

AL - we would like to say something, we do intend to use female pronouns. HH referred to a criminal case and this is not a case of misgendering or use of wrong sex pronouns. Also NC mentions that EW is autistic, I'm not sure of any influence of pronouns on

24 Jun 202610:23

that condition.

NC - correcting a factual point, HH suggested that the the intervenor's advocate using he/him pronouns. It's simply not true, I don't use pronouns when examining witnesses.

J - we are not really concerned with what happened in that tribunal. We will retire.

24 Jun 202610:23

Court rises.

End of morning session part one.

24 Jun 202610:24

@threadreaderapp unroll please.

Wed 24 June AM 2

24 Jun 202610:29

Morning session resumes. https://twitter.com/tribunaltweets/status/2069694223230578706

24 Jun 202610:30

J - with preliminary matters we will give decisions but not extensive reasons. Is that okay?

NC - may depend on the outcome

J - the tribunal will use preferred name and pronouns, C has pc of GRA. As to the Intervenor and witness we understand their position and accept that we

24 Jun 202610:32

cannot compel witness to use specific pronouns, we ask the Intervenor as far as possible to use gender neutral language, we appreciate that this will be difficult, accidental slips may happen, but deliberate use of the wrong term or misgendering will be seen

24 Jun 202610:33

as inappropriate.

NC - can I be completely clear. My witness can use correct pronouns but I cannot?

J - we are asking you and the witness to use gender neutral language,

NC - so is that they/them

J - so they/them and C

NC - I may need to take instruction, can I have your

24 Jun 202610:35

reasons.

J - will give full reasons at the end of the hearing.

J - pausing for a minute.....

J - so, the second issue is I think, the application to amend the list of issues to include section 9.2.

HH - I have hard copies if that would assist.

24 Jun 202610:36

J - am I right to say that C has withdrawn the complaint of direct gender reassignment discrimination, when and how was that done

HH - I believe that was April, I wasn't involved earlier

J - the list of issues in the hearing bundle does not reflect that, there is no amended list

24 Jun 202610:38

of claim, we have spent some time thinking about dir discrim, how did this happen.

HH - I can take instruction on this,

J - I just want to know how this happened

HH - I'll take instructions......

24 Jun 202610:40

HH - I understand the dir discrim claim was withdrawn on 26 April, there was an amended version of partic of claim that was circulated

AL - I have seen an email as of that date, but I haven't seen a formal withdrawal.

J - if someone is withdrawing a claim, it needs to be done

24 Jun 202610:41

clearly and categorically. We have been proceeding on the grounds of claims that are not being made but have disappeared in some mysterious way. that's no way to run a railroad.

HH - my apologies judge.

J - are you happy for us to dismiss that claim.

24 Jun 202610:43

HH - yes

J - are there any other amendment that have been made

AL - the obligation to amend is Cs

HH - I wonder if I might ask the Tribunal for a 5 or 10 minute adjournment to get clarity rather than give piecemeal answer

J - yes, we will take a 10 minute break and then resume.

24 Jun 202610:45

Expecting to resume at 11:55.

24 Jun 202611:05

We resume. There was a hearing, at page 111 para 7 there's reference somewhat cryptically to a number of paras in the original claim..the following amendments are permitted on the grounds either clerical or withdrawal, including para 117, which was the reference to pleaded

24 Jun 202611:07

HH speaking - direct discrimination. And there is an amended part of claim in the amended hearing bundle.

J - may I make a point about the bundle, it's not indexed or the index is separate so we have to scroll at length, would it be possible for someone to index the bundle

24 Jun 202611:08

HH - it was prepared by Rs

J - well, we are having to scroll to page numbers, so if it's page 500 we have to scroll all the way there, which is frustrating and we don't want any frustration....

HH - the direct discrim claim is removed in the update part of claim, it would appear

24 Jun 202611:10

that .

J - it seems that there was no direction to file an updated part of claim, are you happy for us to do that now,

HH - yes, some other minor amendments but nothing substantive

J - so going back to where we started, you've made an app to amend the list of issues

24 Jun 202611:11

HH - the C's application is for a correction of the list of issues

J - a correction of what

HH - the deletion I've referred to in 2024

J - deletion of what, you've made an application to amend the list of issues, am I correct

HH - yes you're right

24 Jun 202611:12

J - it's an application to amend the particulars of claim that is before

HH - I can't invite the tribunal to amend the list of issues when there was an erroneous deletion, I'll expand that

J - AL any comment

AL - well I dont' see how they can get around an application to amend

24 Jun 202611:13

I don't see that she can do it. It's a defective approach, because it does not address the pleadings.

J - it's very confusing, when I first saw this, I wondered if it had been done properly. Are you saying that you now want to attend the part of claim and the list of issues.

24 Jun 202611:14

Amend not attend (auto correct)

HH - yes I think MLF is right and I need to do that. With that background, liability was expressly pleaded, there has been a number of amendments to pleading since then, there was an amended part of claim provided in June 2024, had 2 crucial

24 Jun 202611:16

changes. 1st change was that indiv Rs, Mueller and Wrigley were removed as respondents. In the intro to the amendment that stated it reflected withdrawal of the claim against those two respondents. The 2nd change was that it expanded on the allegations of conduct against those

24 Jun 202611:18

former Rs. The C contends that 1st R & 2nd R are liable for conduct of former 3rd and 4th Rs. Now that para was redlined - struck through. It was a composite paragraph that referred to 3rd and 4th Rs and the liability of of 1st and 2nd Rs. It was an over deletion not a

24 Jun 202611:19

withdrawal of those claims. It makes no sense to withdraw individual claims against AM, EW, include additional allegations against them and then abandon the liability question of 1st and 2nd Rs. When you look at this application, please look at this in context, not an

24 Jun 202611:20

or withdrawal of that basis of liability. I do accept the confusion that has been generated by that earlier deletion of the entire paragraph. I hope you can see how and why that has happened. Because it was a mixed para, the deletion of it is consistent with no claims against

24 Jun 202611:22

AM and EW but not abandonment of corporate liability. Equally there hasn't been a strike out. My primary position - this was an over deletion and the balance of prejudice favours the C, it's not simply sufficient for Rs to plead prejudice where none exists. That is the situation

24 Jun 202611:23

in this case.

J - I think, may I call her Elspeth, may I ask what you are doing, are you using your phone to record

EW - I'm not recording

J - not making any accusation just want to be clear what's happening, no recording

NC - EW is a government lawyer, I'm sure she's aware of

24 Jun 202611:24

that.

J - yes thank you just reminded

HH - back to question of prejudice, Rs have taken no issue with section 109.1 liability, addition of 109.2 involves same acts, same network, same moderation, and witnesses have completely covered this. Even if prejudice does exist, it

24 Jun 202611:26

could be dealt with by additional questions in chief. We are dealing with whether the Rs are responsible for AM, EW. There is no prejudice to Rs. However, there is significant prejudice to the C if these claims cannot be pursued. The balance of prejudice favours the C.

24 Jun 202611:27

HH - dealing with points of Rs in objection, if acts are done within agent's authority, the principal is liable even if not aware and did not approve. In the recent authority from Court of Appeal, no exact legal definition of what is an agent,

24 Jun 202611:29

but that the common law envisages that the agent was acting with the consent of the principal. We can see that was the case. EW and AM both post information as group owners, these things are obvious from the papers and the pleadings. Prejudice to the Rs is nonexistent.

24 Jun 202611:31

AL - the C must plead their case and advance their case, it is not the job of the tribunal to correct an error of the parties seeking the application. It is not a concern of the tribunal or the parties. The C has made various amendments to pleading through a red line process

24 Jun 202611:33

approved by a previous judge. The C went through exactly the same process with red line etc, the process that was taken for the direct discrim pleadings is very different from this application. The explanation that was given beggars belief. And we have no evidence from

24 Jun 202611:35

a solicitor. It's not good enough for C to simply say we didn't mean that, if it was an error, it's still not a good answer. There were further amendments subsequent to that change, there has been discussion at more than one preliminary hearing, there has been so many

24 Jun 202611:36

occasions for this error to be noted, it's astonishing that it has not be spotted until now. Consistently through the list of issues, a consistent line has been taken with respect to vicarious liability is only through section 9.1, employment. C sent updated combined lists of

24 Jun 202611:37

on various dates. Following withdrawal against AM, EW, further lists of issues. All of those lists of issues make no reference to section 9.2. This is why I say even if it can be explained away by an error, there have been numerous opportunities over the years to correct it.

24 Jun 202611:38

The amendment has been proposed today, you have a letter from 10 June that mis-characterised it as an amendment to the list of issues, the list of issues has to reflect the claim. Timing in and of itself, leads into the prejudice point. It's been said against us that Rs cannot

24 Jun 202611:40

blandly plead prejudice that does not exist. That's an astonishing claim to make. Agency is a distinct concept. The Rs have understood liability under 9.1 was at issue and that is how we have prepared our case. Details of relationship between R and arms length bodies would be

24 Jun 202611:41

very important. The 2 individuals are not employed by DEFRA. More details would be needed to prove agency, it can't simply be corrected with a few supplemental questions. The Rs have not had the opportunity to consider the point and would have to deal with it on the hoof,

24 Jun 202611:42

so to speak. The Rs witness statements deal with employment, the Intervenor's witness statement as well. It's said that AM was acting as agent. He's not been called as a witness, he's not a R. We still don't understand what C's case is in this regard. I don't intend to go

24 Jun 202611:44

into the merits, relying on timing and prejudice. The formal announcement of DEFRA SEEN was announced in December 2023, the formal announcement of SEEN CS was made in the Cabinet Officer. Matters of content and moderation happened later. We say it's not blindingly obvious

24 Jun 202611:46

that AM, EW are agents. There is a case to be said to that there is prejudice to Intervenor that the AM and EW are agents of DEFRA and RPA. The prejudice is outweighed by the technical, legal problems. The C has other options here. And if solicitors have made an error,

24 Jun 202611:48

C can have recourse against them.

J - if it is right that AM EW were not employed by Rs, and they cannot rely on 9.2, there cannot be no direct liability. Whether this matters is for us.

AL - needs to look at the claim in its totality, if 9.1 doesn't assist, it might be a

24 Jun 202611:49

problem. The whole claim however does not rest or fall on that. The Tribunal should not look at this as all or nothing, if the C can't take forward those claims it may be a matter of negligence

J - did the Rs ask the C to explain their contention of vicarious liability,

24 Jun 202611:51

AL - reads out page numbers, invited to explain how relevant section 9 applies. It's an obvious opportunity for C to explain how this might apply.

J - let me have a look.

AL - these are amended grounds of resistance

24 Jun 202611:53

AL - the Rs are responding to 109.1, employment and now it goes against the grain to say there is prejudice.

J - do you have anything to say NC

NC - we see a real benefit is seeing all aspects of C's claims being determined in full, our stance remains neutral

J - the Rs might

24 Jun 202611:54

have an interest in 109.2 being explored

AL - that's not a submission I would take forward.

HH - a brief reply, on the question of prejudice, tribunals are invited to focus on the practical consequences of an application, focusing on reality, whether witnesses remember, have

24 Jun 202611:55

records etc about the proposed amendments. The Rs had anticipated the issue that the pleading come first, and you can't simply take the list of issues, the issue of prejudice couldn't have been any surprise. He stated that more detailed evidence as to agency cannot be discounted

24 Jun 202611:57

If that is the height of the objection on prejudice, it falls short. My other point,

J - what is the basis, in it's original form it simply asserts and there is no pleading of the basis on which agency is argued

HH - the factual basis is set out.

J - but it isn't it doesn't set

24 Jun 202611:58

out the connection to agency

HH - its not clearly x-ref'd

J - there has to be some specific assertions about how agency applies

HH - it would not be uncommon for unwanted conduct to have the proscribed effect, we would invite the Tribunal to assume that an agency relationship

24 Jun 202612:00

exists. Further, my instructing solicitors strongly resist that this was anything but an error and the junior solicitor would be prepared to give further evidence.

J - we need to consider our decision. But if we were to allow it, what are the consequences for your case.

24 Jun 202612:01

J - please talk to each other. Don't read anything into that.

AL - not reading anything in to that, disagree with how my submissions were characterised by Cs, I'm now trying to answer the question of how this amendment will impact our case and this hearing.

J - can I ask each

24 Jun 202612:02

of you to consider what you need, do you understand the question.

J - it's 1 o'clock, we'll adjourn until 2 pm, and we'll ask you to address us on that point.

Court rises.

End of morning session part 2.

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

24 Jun 202613:12

In in medias res

HH Hope to provide brief summary in morning. Won't differ much re agency. Eg in relation to EW

J[is difficult to hear] concern is allegation is unpleaded even if amendment allowed. Don't know facts or matters. R doesn't know what case have to meet.

24 Jun 202613:13

J If we grant leave it needs to be pleaded and responded to.

HH Could certainly be put in writing

J What in writing?

HH additions. Don't envisage would mean prejudice in terms of delay, only c half day for R to take instructions. Nothing new from witness statements.

24 Jun 202613:14

AL/ J to and fro

J If amendment allowed, what follows

AL primary subs from MLF doesn't work. Can't proceed without anything else. Re MLF secondary point - is v optimistic this will have little impact and prejudice. We don't have the pleadings yet - we don't know.

24 Jun 202613:16

AL Will need time to analyse. Prospect of delay /derail things. Some things may need to happen. Potentially need to undertake document search, further witness statements.. I just don't know but prejudice does exist.

J This isn't ;personal, but you did have c two weeks notice

24 Jun 202613:17

AL Difficulty is that this is raised when focus of parties is preparing for 3 week trial. This isn't in the pleaded case or list of issues. My sub is would be counsel of perfection to assume R would have

J I wasn't meaning ...If it wasn't done, just say that.

24 Jun 202613:19

AL But raises q of what should R have been doing in the time, and also the C could have ... criticism cuts both ways.

J We need to decide what to do. Will take a little time. Will need to leave as will need docs with us. Will be half an hour minimum

24 Jun 202613:20

NC Have been asked to mention that observers on remote link can't hear you, Judge, v clearly

J Thank you for telling me. Wasn't aware of that. Thank you.

Wed 24 June PM 2

24 Jun 202613:27

Second afternoon session, day one, of the employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest v Defra and the Rural Payments Agency. We expect to return some time after 2.50pm.

24 Jun 202614:09

J This is our unanimous decision on C application to amend claim. C applies to inc allegation that Rs are liable under section 109 2 for actions of EW and AM - actions done as agents for R. Application R liable was first made in 2023. Remains a boiler plate contention

24 Jun 202614:10

J Removal of original Rs in 2024 and tribunal gave leave to amend. Para 58 removed entirely - HH says was an error. Thereafter draft list of issues went through amendments and error not realised. therefore no argument in pleading or list that section 109 still relied on

24 Jun 202614:12

J C asked to amend. HH appreciated also need to amend claim to include sec 109, but list of issues is parasitic upon particulars of claim.

[NC and others dealing with tech]

NC Apologies. Sorry to interrupt you Judge. Was trying to do this discreetly.

J That didn't work (!)

24 Jun 202614:13

J How tribunal deals with requests to amend claim. Balance of prejudice - Tribunal should take realistic and real world approach as to what happens if amendment allowed or not. Tribunal don't accept HH view that Rs should have realised re section 109 that it had bee removed.

24 Jun 202614:15

J tribunal doesn't consider that Rs are being opportunistic.

Application to amend has been made extremely late. Allegation isn't particularised - this needs to be done and further docs/witness statements would be needed. Also here, arms'-length bodies involved.

24 Jun 202614:16

J If granted, proceedings would have to be delayed,

Tribunal concludes unanimously in service of justice. that application to amend claim is refused. This is final decision. Reasons will be fully set out.

24 Jun 202614:17

J Nature of allegations in this case mean best to deal with this as a liability hearing.

[counsel agree]

J No more to be said about that, then. Would be helpful if we could have revised agreed list of issues. Don't need a shortened version.

NC I prepared the shorter list

24 Jun 202614:18

J no offence

NC none taken! Not the length or formulation of doc which will cause difficulty if we stick with the long version but the numbering. [gives eg]

J I have your point. When I was preparing this I renumbered the list of issues completely and then realised

24 Jun 202614:20

that would cause complete chaos.

AL Appreciated it was done to help not hinder.

J Can I suggest we have amended list of issues and an abridged list of issues so we can work with those henceforth.

HH I'm happy to lead on this if it will assist.

24 Jun 202614:21

HH Re specific allegations made against EW and Muller. C will take a view on that and revert to tribunal.

HH Depending on option chosen, you (judge) have indicated within list of issues is failure to moderate certain behaviours later on.

J I didn't say moderate but that is

24 Jun 202614:22

part of what I was thinking. Rs failed to act re posts that were made, and that is still part of allegations

HH Yes

J Is there any issues still between parties re docs.

HH You've seen applications. We say all docs are relevant for X exam. Re Intervenors application -

24 Jun 202614:24

HH I am aware of time. To make an application of such length and WSs exchanged late in the day [early June]

25 Jun 202608:40

This is the morning session, day two, of the employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest v DEFRA and the Rural Payments Agency. We expect to begin at 10am.

25 Jun 202608:42

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

25 Jun 202608:42

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

25 Jun 202608:43

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

…tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/introduction

25 Jun 202608:44

Abbreviations:

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

25 Jun 202608:45

SEEN/IP – Sex Equality and Equity Network – Civil Service

EW – Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC – Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as Intervenor

J – Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 – Panel member 1

P2 – Panel member 2

AL – Alex Line, barrister for Rs

25 Jun 202608:45

JRL – JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

HH – Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

AB – Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant

AM – Andreas Mueller

GC – gender critical or sex realist views

GI – gender identity

25 Jun 202608:46

TW – transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment TM – transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment

INT – Intervenor

ETBB – Equal Treatment Bench Book

GRC – Gender recognition certificate

PC – Protected characteristic

25 Jun 202608:46

GRA – PC of gender reassignment

DD – Direct discrimination

MLF – My learned friend

to rebut that, C would be at disadvantage cos we would say tribunal may be misled. The problems of 900 page bundle at 11th hour.

AL: p732 please

ST: I wanted to say more about Caroline's statement?

25 Jun 202613:04

25 Jun 202613:05

ST: Yes but 488

AL: You were not on the moderation panel?

ST: No

25 Jun 202613:06

AL: p732

AL: This is from a slide pack, 8/3/23, we can see that Yammer will be migrating to native late April. We agree there's no substantive difference we need to consider in the Yammer versions?

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:07

AL: Says that whole-company won't be available any more, only Yammer groups. If you are member of a group you will see convos, if not not

25 Jun 202613:08

ST: At this time all groups were public and it was a bit like FB giving you a feed.

AL: Indeed yes, in March, but, intention was to move away from that after April?

ST: You would still get feed from groups not in.

25 Jun 202613:08

AL: But that reduced after the April changes?

ST: Don't know how it decided what came up.

25 Jun 202613:09

J: Before April everything was all-company?

ST: No, groups too

J: But all public?

ST: Yes

J: So everything was on "all company"?

ST: No, but it was the default starting page.

25 Jun 202613:10

J: But after April, how could you see things that are in private groups you're not in?

ST: As I understand it, like FB, you could get stuff in feed from any public group - things it thought you would be interested in

25 Jun 202613:11

J: Is that bcs the group is public, would it happen if group is private?

ST: No if private group, only members see it.

J: So if *all* groups were private, you'd only see your own?

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:12

AL: So we are in March/April 2023. Page 767.

AL: at top, DEFRA group principles of communication - version is dated March 2023. Next page - explicit bullet point says Yammer included

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:13

AL: Also references to other docs eg code of conduct and acceptable use, and stresses that this doc complements those. Must be read alongside, yes?

ST: Would expect them to be included

AL: But it *says*, amplifies and supplements?

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:15

AL: If you look at the highlighted bits - version control, this is March 2023 changes/additions. p770 - under 'freedom of expression'.

25 Jun 202613:15

AL: You can see the version we looked at earlier is still there, but there's an addition - nobody has freedom from offence, but at work bear in mind professional standards. Correct no right not to be offended?

ST: Disagree

AL: No right in law?

ST: Agree

25 Jun 202613:16

AL: This section is aiming to balance freedom of expression with professional standards - rights of speaker and hearer - yes?

ST: Agree in principle but I don't think it's being applied correctly.

25 Jun 202613:17

AL: p771 - there are statements following those principles. And examples of application - not intended to be exhaustive. Says, it is not acceptable to characterise a lawfully held belief as inherently discriminatory. This is correct isn't it?

25 Jun 202613:18

ST: I think it's contradictory. Says nobody has a right never to be offended and then says you can't call something offensive.

AL: Emphasis is on it being a belief legally protected in law, which cannot be inherently discriminatory or offensive.

25 Jun 202613:19

ST: I disagree, if we have to work together -

J: Can we slow down for note-taking?

ST: I think, times when we work together - let me try to remember my words - I think, times when it's right to limit expression to foster better working together.

25 Jun 202613:20

AL: The expression of a lawfully held belief? Can be lawfully restrained, you mean gender-critical beliefs?

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:21

AL: But that is not what this section is about - it's about saying such a belief is inherently offensive / discriminatory. For example someone simply saying they have GC beliefs?

J: This is a complicated Q - can you break it down.

25 Jun 202613:23

AL: I think I will have to check my notes - please bear with me. I want to be careful I don't end up asking ST a different question. [is reading notes etc]

25 Jun 202613:24

AL: I asked ST, are you talking about GC beliefs etc, and that as they are legally protected beliefs they cannot be inherently discriminatory can they and (I thought) ST agreed, and that this was what the section we are looking at says, and I thought ST had agreed.

25 Jun 202613:25

ST: Yes

25 Jun 202613:25

ST: I disagree, I think you can put limits on the expression of the belief it's conducive to working relations between staff.

25 Jun 202613:27

J: Your view is, it's appropriate to limit even the mere expression of *holding* a belief, for good relations?

AL: Yes that is what I'm trying to ask.

25 Jun 202613:28

ST: I still say, negative effect in the workplace - should not be mentioned, stay away from it in the workplace.

J: Your proposition is that the mere articulation of GC belief is discrimintory?

ST: Yes, it erases transgender ppl

25 Jun 202613:28

J: And that's the opposite of what this doc says - that legally held beliefs cannot be discriminatory?

ST: Yes I think so - you can believe things, but, discriminatory to mention in the workplace.

25 Jun 202613:29

J: Inherently. That's an absolute. However expressed, whatever is said.

ST: Yes

AL: There should never be mention in the workplace?

ST: Perhaps if discussing a policy, but, never just by staff.

25 Jun 202613:30

AL: So subject to limited exception, your view on that is absolutist?

ST: Yes. It causes harassment.

25 Jun 202613:31

25 Jun 202613:31

25 Jun 202613:32

25 Jun 202613:33

@threadreaderapp please unroll

Fri 26 June AM 1

26 Jun 202608:34

Good morning. This is the first morning session on day three of the employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest v DEFRA and the Rural Payments Agency. We expect to begin at 10am.

26 Jun 202608:40

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

26 Jun 202608:41

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

26 Jun 202608:41

Our Substack page on the case contains all the previous sessions, and a full list of abbreviations

tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

26 Jun 202608:44

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs. Please consider subscribing.

26 Jun 202608:45

Abbreviations:

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

26 Jun 202608:46

SEEN/IP – Sex Equality and Equity Network – Civil Service

EW – Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC – Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as Intervenor

J – Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 – Panel member 1

P2 – Panel member 2

AL – Alex Line, barrister for Rs

26 Jun 202608:46

JRL – JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

HH – Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

AB – Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant

AM – Andreas Mueller

GC – gender critical or sex realist views

GI – gender identity

26 Jun 202608:46

TW – transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment TM – transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment

INT – Intervenor

ETBB – Equal Treatment Bench Book

GRC – Gender recognition certificate

PC – Protected characteristic

26 Jun 202608:47

GRA – PC of gender reassignment

DD – Direct discrimination

MLF – My learned friend

CA – Caroline Airs

ED – Emma Dunn

NB – Nicola Bettesworth

VE – Viva Engage

CM – Carly Manning

IAO – Information Asset Owners

NE – Natural England

26 Jun 202608:47

OH – Occupational health

G – Grievance

GA – Grievance appeal

GI – Grievance investigation/investigator

SGI – Second grievance investigation

SGA – Second grievance appeal

midst of X-exam can forget.

26 Jun 202609:39

26 Jun 202609:41

by Forstater.

26 Jun 202609:47

26 Jun 202609:49

talking to staff members.

AL So can you then see why one can't

26 Jun 202609:50

say eg SEEN network can't exist, as may be the network to feed into decisions like that.

26 Jun 202609:52

feeding in views.

26 Jun 202609:54

[all agree]

team do

26 Jun 202609:58

C I see it does mention the Equalities Act

AL Reads c PSED, by encouraging diverse views and tolerant open dialogue. Objectives of SEEN presented - clear they didn't seek to eradicate t people from work place

26 Jun 202609:59

C don't agree Can pick up they wanted Civil Service based around sex-based rights, that sex is binary and immutable. These statement at face value would erase t people.

Various views - opening up t people to problems. Would cause t people ultimately to fade away from workplace.

26 Jun 202610:00

C If you take SEEN principles - you would lose language, pronouns, gi. Without those t people would no longer exist in workplace. Not true they are looking to encourage good relations and diversity of voices - is all geared towards removing that diversity.

26 Jun 202610:02

26 Jun 202610:03

C Not fair characterisation. Don't want my existence to be debated in the workplace and not to be removed from the workplace.

26 Jun 202610:15

26 Jun 202612:40

Our full coverage of the case can be found on our Substack tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

26 Jun 202612:40

The court is at present on lunch break, and will resume at 2pm. Alexander Line (AL) barrister for Respondents DEFRA & RPA will continue his cross-examination of Samantha Tempest (ST) the Claimant.

26 Jun 202613:15

26 Jun 202613:21

26 Jun 202613:22

26 Jun 202613:41

26 Jun 202613:46

ST: Yes

26 Jun 202613:47

26 Jun 202613:48

ST: ANd SEEN posts are not necessarily in their own group - so I might see them - so, convos in I think it wsa menopause / breastfeeding groups - nothing to do with SEEN but they post - and I still get notifications, I haven't been removed form everything, which is astounding.

26 Jun 202613:49

AL: The examples of claimed harassment in 2nd grievance - those were the only ones you had. Bcs not being exposed to content you wd find offensive?

26 Jun 202613:50

AL: You were not exposed to any SEEN content, to be clear

26 Jun 202613:50

26 Jun 202613:51

26 Jun 202613:53

26 Jun 202613:54

26 Jun 202613:54

26 Jun 202613:55

26 Jun 202613:56

26 Jun 202613:57

26 Jun 202613:57

26 Jun 202613:59

26 Jun 202613:59

26 Jun 202614:01

26 Jun 202614:02

26 Jun 202614:03

26 Jun 202614:04

AL: Yes

26 Jun 202614:05

26 Jun 202614:06

26 Jun 202614:07

26 Jun 202614:07

26 Jun 202614:08

26 Jun 202614:09

Mon 29 June AM 1

29 Jun 202608:43

This is the beginning of the second week in the hearing at employment tribunal Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency.

29 Jun 202608:43

Our Substack page about the case, including reporting of the first week of the hearing, is here tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

29 Jun 202608:43

We expect the hearing to begin at 10.00am.

29 Jun 202608:43

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

29 Jun 202608:43

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

29 Jun 202608:43

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs. Please consider subscribing tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

29 Jun 202608:43

A full list of abbreviations can be found on the Substack page.

Today the Claimant (C) Samantha Tempest (ST) will be further cross-examined by Alexander Line (AL), barrister for both the Respondents.

29 Jun 202609:10

[We are waiting for the session to begin]

29 Jun 202609:12

[We begin - HH counsel for the Claimant is asking to be able to consult with her client, this is not usually allowed while a witness is mid-evidence so HH has to ask the court]

29 Jun 202609:13

[The court is discussing releasing documents about the case and HH would like to talk to C about any potential privacy concerns re this]

29 Jun 202609:16

HH = Helen Hogben KC, counsel for the Claimant

AL = Alexander Line, counsel for the Respondents

NC = Naomi Cunningham, counsel for the Intervenor (SEEN)

29 Jun 202609:17

[The hearing is on a short break to allow HH to take instructions from C]

29 Jun 202609:33

[The public session has still not restarted]

29 Jun 202609:41

[We resume]

29 Jun 202609:42

HH: In relation to shd tribunal provide electronic copy of bundle - obviously starting point is open justice - done and seen to be done, public scrutiny is accountability.

29 Jun 202609:43

HH: C entirely accepts that, however, Q is does OpenJ require full bundle to be issued. [Cites court rules] - deals with WS and yes make available electronically if necessary, but, does not deal with full bundle.

29 Jun 202609:44

HH: We say, that's bcs physical bundle in room is under court control, but, that's not true of an electronic version.

29 Jun 202609:45

HH: This case is much public interest but also sensitive personal info, so we argue for controlled release of some but not all documents. Q is, what access is necessary - fair and proportionate - to support public scrutiny.

29 Jun 202609:46

HH: We say, best achieved by keeping the full bundle under tribunal control. 2022 guidance from Lord Chief Justice re remote hearings - recognises that dissemination by remote observers more likely.

29 Jun 202609:47

HH: [case ref from 2022 involving Guardian newspaper]

J: checking we have copies of that - what para

HH: 107

29 Jun 202609:48

HH: Guardian applied for specific docs after a hearing, tribunal initially refused, EAT said that was wrong and some particular docs shd be released. [case is Rozanov ?spelling]

29 Jun 202609:49

HH: Ruling notes that a lot of bundle information may be irrelevant, and even parts of a doc discussed in cart the rest of the doc might not be relevant.

29 Jun 202609:50

HH: Clear that putting whole caboodle in public domain is not necessarily desirable. NB it's not just the C - other witnesses, cd be personal information eg email addresses, or sensitive data.

29 Jun 202609:50

HH: Simply disclosing entire bundle is not the right answer, so, C strongly resists the dissemination of the whole bundle.

29 Jun 202609:51

HH: Ppl should apply for particular docs and tribunal can consider that then.

J: What about issuing the final version of claim, of response, and the list of issues.

HH: [unsure]

29 Jun 202609:53

HH: I think providing those documents would help understanding of the case?

J: I feel that full bundle will contain so much that is not relevant, would be a distraction really

29 Jun 202609:54

HH: Just to summarise Rozanov, the grant made was only of certain specific documents.

HH: Also must point out that adequate redaction of bundle wd be a massive task

29 Jun 202609:55

AL: We are a public body so no Article 8 rights unlike C, but, yes there is lots of email addresses and phone numbers of our staff in there, so the proportionality of the request made - this is discussed in Rozanov

29 Jun 202609:56

AL: So yes we echo the points made re proportionality. Re doing some docs - the pleadings we can't see that there is a problem issuing those, and tend to agree that if specific docs are requested ppl can ask for those they want

29 Jun 202609:57

NC: For Intervenor - we agree that pleadings and list of issues would not be a problem [missed a little bit]

29 Jun 202609:58

J: There have been several applications - none very detailed or much argued. We will take 5 minutes to discuss among ourselves.

[SHORT BREAK]

29 Jun 202609:59

29 Jun 202610:01

This is part 2 of the morning session in Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency; the first part of the morning hearing is https://x.com/tribunaltweets/status/2071514906864554280?s=20

29 Jun 202610:01

The court is at present taking a short break for the Judge and Panel members to decide whether to grant press requests for access to an electronic of the tribunal's evidence bundle.

29 Jun 202610:03

J: We are minded to agree to electronic disclosure of the pleadings and the agreed list of issues - we think, not remotely controversial and will be v helpful to press understanding the case.

29 Jun 202610:03

J: We are *not* minded to agree to release the full bundle.

29 Jun 202610:04

J: Re disclosure of witness statements - we place here in the room once each is sworn, we feel that electronic version for press at the same point of time is also OK

29 Jun 202610:05

J: So we are not going to make any order for electronic disclosure beyond that. If press want any further disclosure must make formal written application with reasons etc for us to consider.

29 Jun 202610:06

J: Please note that it's for the parties to do the disclosing - if you could arrange this between you. I don't think onerous.

AL: I will take instructions but am sure we can work something out.

HH: If I could also just take instruction from C on that briefly?

29 Jun 202610:07

J: Recognise that ppl who applied for this will be listening - want to say that I shall issue a written order, tomorrow morning at the latest, but meanwhile we will get on with things.

29 Jun 202610:08

J: And remind press that the material is only for the purposes of reporting this tribunal. AL, HH do take instructions re witness statements.

29 Jun 202610:08

J: We will break here for you to take instructions and resume at 11.20

[BREAK]

29 Jun 202610:31

[We are still waiting for the public hearing to restart]

29 Jun 202610:36

[We begin]

29 Jun 202610:37

HH: Have taken instruction from C - it appears position would be academic as WS already issued to tribunaltweets, I understand by intervenor, it is lucky that C (and other witness ED) have no objections to their WS being issued.

29 Jun 202610:38

HH: Wish to raise: have been shown a tweet from intervenor solicitor (Levins) on the firm's page what looks like a screenshot of EDW post of 9/7/2022. No order from tribunal for that.

29 Jun 202610:39

HH: All parties should be aware that anything made public requires an order from tribunal.

29 Jun 202610:40

J: Miss Cunningham?

NC: I think I knew that intervenor's statement had been tweeted yes.

J: From bundle?

NC: C has had the document -

J: Not my Q - what was the source?

NC: I don't know what solicitor used I'm afraid.

29 Jun 202610:41

J: This case is attracting a lot of interest and there's obviously a lot of conflict - this was not helpful. I don't want to say too much; anxious to do what we are here to do. But I do ask parties to be circumspect; we can't control what parties do.

29 Jun 202610:43

J: Am not making any accusations. We don't know how this happens.

NC: Implied criticism of solicitor - wd be grateful if made clear so he knows not to do it again.

J: Will reflect on that. Unwise to comment immediately; we will come back to it if necessary.

29 Jun 202610:44

J: Let's get on

NC: I do have one more point - HH said it was unfortunate the WS had been released. I raised this explicitly on Friday [reads from tribunaltweets] saying in public domain once sworn.

J: Let's not waste any more time.

29 Jun 202610:45

AL: One small practical matter - re the C supplementary bundle, just want to check you have it in the form you need it, because I'll be referring to it today.

29 Jun 202610:46

HH: We had not been given a DUC link this morning - let me check if we have had that. No we haven't had one.

J: Who needs it? C's solicitors?

HH: Yes

29 Jun 202610:46

J: Clerks are sorting out. I am sorry to seem so frustrated, will sort this out now.

29 Jun 202610:47

[short pause for getting the additional bundle pages to everyone that needs them]

29 Jun 202610:54

J: Docs are on their way.

AL: Thank you judge.

29 Jun 202610:55

HH: One more point - re failing to moderate Andreas Mueller, you'll remember that there had been additional disclosure re that, I have paper copies and will upload to DUC too - all parties happy to admit.

29 Jun 202610:56

[HH handing out paper copies of the additional material]

HH: Two sections - docs from C, docs from R, the larger is the C's.

29 Jun 202610:57

J: No objections to these?

AL: We made no objections provided ours were accepted too.

29 Jun 202610:58

AL: ST we are going to go through various issues of harassment etc 1.1(xii) in list of issues.

AL: p1791

29 Jun 202610:59

AL: Article "why SEEN is important to me". You relied on several parts of this in your second grievance.

29 Jun 202611:00

AL: p1792 author talks about accepting gender identity ideology being like having to follow grandparent religion; later, that GII forces author to lie; and that it worries author re boundaries of private spaces eg toilets.

29 Jun 202611:01

ST: Yes those are what I object to.

AL: Do you accept, private thoughts of a lesbian writer talking about herself and own feelings.

ST: Really she's writing about trans ppl, rejecting legitimacy of trans ppl

29 Jun 202611:02

AL: She say SEEN was like a breath of fresh air - that's about how she feels, not trans ppl

ST: Yes she's writing about that but also about rejecting trans ppl and legitimacy.

29 Jun 202611:02

AL: Only first name given and that writer is a civil servant. Need not be DEFRA?

ST: No

AL: Published on the CS-wide website, not DEFRA?

ST: Published yes

29 Jun 202611:03

J: Can I check, you accept it was the cross-CS SEEN?

ST: Doesn't mean it wasn't promoted within DEFRA

29 Jun 202611:04

AL: p823

AL: Email from Sarah McNally to someone called Jason, Cabinet Office, and others. Discussing receipt of complaint about that article?

Mon 29 June PM 1

29 Jun 202612:47

The tribunal of Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency will resume this afternoon. The Judge mentioned that they may not be back as scheduled at 2 pm. They hope to examine the moderation log (large EXCEL spreadsheet) before resumption.

29 Jun 202612:48

Our previous coverage can be found on our Substack here:

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

29 Jun 202612:48

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

29 Jun 202612:49

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs. Please consider subscribing.

29 Jun 202612:49

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

29 Jun 202612:51

Earlier sessions have included discussions of pronouns and how the C is to be referred to. The judge has issued directions. We have copied this from @JRLevinsLaw X post, therefore in the public domain.

29 Jun 202612:52

@JRLevinsLaw Abbreviations:

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

29 Jun 202612:53

@JRLevinsLaw SEEN/IP – Sex Equality and Equity Network – Civil Service

EW – Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC – Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as Intervenor

J – Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 – Panel member 1

P2 – Panel member 2

AL – Alex Line, barrister for Rs

29 Jun 202612:53

@JRLevinsLaw JRL – JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

HH – Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

AB – Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant

AM – Andreas Mueller

GC – gender critical or sex realist views

GI – gender identity

29 Jun 202612:53

@JRLevinsLaw TW – transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment TM – transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment

INT – Intervenor

ETBB – Equal Treatment Bench Book

GRC – Gender recognition certificate

PC – Protected characteristic

29 Jun 202612:54

GRA – PC of gender reassignment

DD – Direct discrimination

MLF – My learned friend

ED – Emma Dunn

CM – Carly Manning

IAO – Information Asset Owners

NE – Natural England

29 Jun 202612:54

OH – Occupational health

G – Grievance

GA – Grievance appeal

GI – Grievance investigation/investigator

SGI – Second grievance investigation

SGA – Second grievance appeal

30 Jun 202608:56

Link to our previous coverage is here:

tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

30 Jun 202608:58

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

30 Jun 202608:58

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

30 Jun 202608:59

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

30 Jun 202609:01

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

Good afternoon; this is the afternoon session on 30th June of Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency at employment tribunal.

30 Jun 202612:47

Unfortunately we mistook a break the court took at 12.05 as being an hour long (for lunch) when in fact it was a ten minute mid-session break; this means that our coverage misses out the part of the hearing from about 12.15-12.45 today. We apologise.

30 Jun 202612:47

We are expecting the afternoon session to being at 2.05pm.

30 Jun 202612:51

Naomi Cunningham (NC) will begin cross examination of the claimant Samantha Tempest (ST) on behalf of case intervenor SEEN (Sex Equality & Equity Network) and its representative Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley (EDW)

30 Jun 202613:14

NC: No will come to it later - poss not till tomorrow

30 Jun 202613:15

J: We really are working under a lot of pressure on staff - not an excuse, but just to note.

30 Jun 202613:15

NC: Am informed that witness Emma Dunn has been visible attempting to communicate with the claimant - nodding and mouthing - wd ask that ED be asked to change seats so this is made harder.

30 Jun 202613:16

HH: Do not believe there has been any such attempt.

J: I have noticed that ED does have lots of facial expression - that might be being misunderstood -

30 Jun 202613:17

J: I am not accusing at all, but this really must not happen.

HH: We do give advice & warnings to witnesses re this - ED is a v expressive person - she will try to control this.

30 Jun 202613:18

J: Please do; we will otherwise have to do something about this.

HH: Must note that this is not first attempt to mischaracterise ED.

NC: I dispute this; this is something I have been told about, clearly I cannot see as ED is behind me.

30 Jun 202613:19

NC: Finally - pronouns - I understood I could either use ST preferred pronouns or avoid, the written order says use "they/them" - need to know if can do my preference and simply avoid pronouns

J: Yes that is entirely acceptable.

30 Jun 202613:20

NC: Good afternoon, am Qing on behalf of the intervenor. I have moved to this end to encourage you to keep voice up as I'm firther away.

30 Jun 202613:21

NC: You said this morning you wanted to correct some evidence, wd like to let you do that now.

ST: p1860 - I said re Steve Moore decision, this is the draft. He upholds my grievance - I think I had said he didn't.

30 Jun 202613:21

ST: Begins 1859. [All are reading]

30 Jun 202613:23

J: email before it p1857?

ST: yes. IMO from the draft, Steve M upholds my complaint, the environment is toxic for me, he makes recommendations, but in the email it seems to say he can't get DEFRA agreement.

30 Jun 202613:23

ST: This contrasts with final letter - very different from draft - but initially he upheld my complaint, so I believe he was pressured to change it.

30 Jun 202613:24

NC: A few Qs re SEEN's purpose. What do you think is main purpose?

ST: That sex is binary and immutable, and about advanceing equality between men and women.

30 Jun 202613:25

NC: p356. First 3 objectives. You said, "sex is binary and immutable & equality men-women". Entirely clear here that equality is the primary obj?

ST: That's what it says yes

30 Jun 202613:26

NC: There are other networks re 'gender equality'

ST: Yes

NC: But fully signed up to gender ideology?

ST: Wouldn't put it like that. And I don't speak for them.

30 Jun 202613:27

NC: p874 (and look also at 870). This is doc on lines to take about SEEN by Caroline Airs, approved by Nicola Bettesworth?

ST: Don't know who drafted or approved it.

NC: I think doc can speak for itself.

30 Jun 202613:28

NC: Then there's an update and on 874 we see that SEEN concentrates on protected characteristic of sex, and that this is different from the gender or LGBTQ networks. SEEN is unique in this?

30 Jun 202613:28

ST: Disagree, there are GC members of LGBTQ network - don't know about gender one - their views are as much represneted as anyone else

NC: You are saying equally represented by those?

ST: Yes

30 Jun 202613:29

NC: Explain?

ST: Well GC members in LGBTQ made views known and network was much more neutral after that.

30 Jun 202613:31

NT: p1303. Sarah Homer is v senior in DEFRA?

ST: yes

NC: She says - I imagine same on all all - email sig that she is sponsor of the Pride and Gender networks?

ST: Yes

30 Jun 202613:31

NC: Is the implication not that she thinks women's rights (per biology) don't need separate championing?

ST: Disagree

NC: B4 SEEN there was no such representation

30 Jun 202613:32

ST: There was a women's network, changed its name to gender network

NC: Surely that means, it's for women - unless they identify as men - plus men who identify as women?

ST: I don't know enough about it to say.

30 Jun 202613:33

ST: All I know is, women's network changed its name to gender network.

30 Jun 202613:34

NC: Surely tribunal can infer you would know if there had been the slightest GC statement from 'gender' network, because if it had all hell would have broken loose as when EDW posted?

30 Jun 202613:34

ST: Don't agree, EDW posted on all-company

NC: We would know if they had, surely?

ST: Disagree

30 Jun 202613:35

NC: So the group - women (unless TM) plus TW - that is not a coherent category is it?

ST: That depends what kind of policy you are trying to make. Can be appropriate to have inclusive language saying several things

30 Jun 202613:36

NC: Biological sex is a coherent category - you can make law and policy on that basis. But if you muddle - put some men into W category and some women in M, not coherent?

30 Jun 202613:37

ST: I think that is what the law allows - I am not sure what you are asking me?

NC: The networks should be based on coherent, prob legal categories? No M can identify into F category?

J: That is 2 Qs really - break it up?

30 Jun 202613:38

NC: Point of networks is, to group together ppl who have shared needs, priorities?

ST: Wd say that is part of it, but networks also try to bring together anyone interesting in *supporting* their objectives.

30 Jun 202613:39

ST: It's not in a vacuum like that.

NC: True that biological women do have somethings in common that no man can share, because he's not a woman?

30 Jun 202613:39

ST: Don't know what you are alluding to. Something "salient in common" - pls clarify?

NC: Women have biologically female bodies and hence shared needs.

ST: Don't accept.

30 Jun 202613:40

NC: You said yesterday, SEEN had never posted re equality between men and women.

ST: Not to my knowledge,

NC: p1267

30 Jun 202613:41

NC: Heading is what is DEFRA SEEN about - the intro post. First line is, promote equality and equity between men and women, post about men and women?

ST: No it's just a statement.

30 Jun 202613:43

NC: Intervenor bundle p19 [I think]

NC: Do you accept this about men and women? Ending VAWG?

ST: No

NC: Why not?

ST: Exclusionary to trans ppl, removes us from included in that.

30 Jun 202613:44

NC: I think you are saying that bcs SEEN focuses on biological women, it never posts about women or sex equality, because they would need to talk about W+TW?

ST: Every post goes on about excluding trans ppl.

30 Jun 202613:44

NC: Did you read this article?

J: If I may - p19, ST you say, this excludes TW - where is there exclusion? Other than it being SEEN

30 Jun 202613:45

ST: I think their website was hidden at this time, there was a Viva Engage post though, and SEEN members made it very clear they did not include TW. Don't believe they changed their position for this article.

30 Jun 202613:46

NC: p866 (main)

NC: Second paragraph beginning "in relation to" - can you read that pls

ST [reads]

30 Jun 202613:47

NC: This is re where SEEN fits conceptually

ST: Yes

NC: Caroline A is saying the point is not the belief, the focus is on actual sex. That's right isn't it?

ST: Yes agree focus of SEEN is sex.

30 Jun 202613:49

NC: p546 (int)

NC: Post in SEEN network by EDW - says look forward to world without sex stereotypes, where boys and girls can achieve what they want to do. Shares resources on challenging sex stereotypes?

30 Jun 202613:50

ST: Um - no.

NC: How do you characterise.

ST: Have said before. These posts have veneer of respectability and neutrality, but in fact, like other posts, delegitimises gender identity.

30 Jun 202613:50

NC: Do you agree that on its face, the post is sharing resources challenging sexist stereotypes?

ST: Don't think it challenges anything, it's just a statement.

30 Jun 202613:51

NC: What is it in that post makes you think it delegitimises gender identity?

ST: It's dealing in gender expression, saying a man can wear a dress and have long hair and still be a man, that is not my lived experiences of gender identity.

30 Jun 202613:52

ST: Offensive to say I am cosplaying women. I *am* a woman.

30 Jun 202613:53

NC: Are you saying it's not possible to have long hair and wear a dress and be a man?

ST: Yes

NC: Some men do?

ST: yes

NC: Are you delegitimising *their* lived experience?

ST: No.

30 Jun 202613:53

NC: You say this post has a hidden meaning that delegitmises trans ppl

ST: Yes

30 Jun 202613:54

NC: You said you were removed from many groups to avoid upset

ST: Yes

NC: You said that SEEN still posted in menopause and breastfeeding groups. Are you saying those are not to do with sex?

30 Jun 202613:55

ST: Up to them what they post.

NC: Not up to you?

ST: No

NC: Did you join those groups.

ST: Looked at them yes

[missed - re TM also involved]

30 Jun 202613:56

NC: But you cannot say those issues are nothing to do with sex?

ST: So say menopause, SEEN will have a position / interest from PoV of sex, but they rigidly stick to it, so other ppl there - say TM and TW with legit interest, they are being told they are not part of group

30 Jun 202613:57

ST: And often they are only posting about SEEN network, which is nothing to do with menopause. Just trying to recruit members.

30 Jun 202613:58

NC: The menopause group is nothing to with trans identify men - transwomen. They don't go through menopause.

ST: Not accurate. We may have loved ones that do.

30 Jun 202613:58

ST: And when you have gender surgery and stop taking hormones you get hot flushes and brain fog, so yes TW belong.

30 Jun 202613:59

NC: Menopause is the cessation of ovulation

ST: Main group yes

NC: Men never ovulate, so they never stop.

ST: Agree TW don't ovulate.

NC: So don't go through menopause

ST: Well if you define it like that, but I have described my experenience of menopause symptoms.

30 Jun 202614:00

ST: And even a TW who doesn't have symptoms doesn't mean they should not be in the group

30 Jun 202614:01

NC: Similarly TW can't breastfeed.

ST: Yes they can

NC: True that some TW take drugs to induce a secretion, and have babies suck nipples? That's not breasteeding is it

ST: It is to me

30 Jun 202614:02

NC: Is it OK for a TW to join a group for mutual support of breastfeeding?

ST: As I say ppl join groups to support loved ones too, so yes

30 Jun 202614:02

30 Jun 202614:03

30 Jun 202614:04

NC: It's explicit

ST: Suppose

30 Jun 202614:05

30 Jun 202614:05

30 Jun 202614:07

J: Last note I have is re EDW posts?

NC: Yes - and we agreed, personal not SEEN

ST: not sure - agree hadn't posted

NC: And you didn't know about it at that time?

ST: That's right.

30 Jun 202614:08

NC: para 24 of your WS - you say, when you saw post you were v upset - if ppl like SEEN say sex is binary & immutable they are saying ppl like you are unwell / indoctrinated if they don't subscribe to gender identity.

30 Jun 202614:09

NC: Where does the post say there is no such thing as gender identity?

ST: Well if you say sex is binary & immutable, that is the outcome.

30 Jun 202614:10

NC: It's not actually saying there is no such thing as GI, it's saying that if / when it comes to being male for female mattering, it's sex (not GI) that matters.

ST: I don't agree with that position.

30 Jun 202614:10

NC: I'm not asking you to agree the position, I'm asking you to agree what the post says.

ST: Have already said how I read that post, can't add more I think

30 Jun 202614:11

NC: You say that SEEN existence, that post, GC views, frame your existence as a debate. That's something you've picked up?

ST: No it's what I think

30 Jun 202614:12

NC: Hyperbolic, isn't it? Nobody debating "existence", only what consequences there are?

ST: Disagree, not hyperbolic, it all basically means whether ppl like me exist

30 Jun 202614:12

NC: The discussion is only about what consequences *your* GI should have for *other* people?

ST: That is part of it but not all of it.

30 Jun 202614:13

NC: Suppose I say I am indecisive, I'm Leo with the moon in Gemini? You might say, "I don't believe in astrology" and I say "Ah well agree to disagree" and we go back to work. Isn't that a better model?

30 Jun 202614:14

ST: Disagree, astrology not contentious, but if it is contentious don't raise it at all

NC: Don't you understand that your claim to be a woman is contentious to some?

ST: I don't post about it, I just want to go to work and do my job

30 Jun 202614:15

NC: I've suggested that better way of proceeding is agree to differ. If I said that you not believing in astrology denies my existence - that would be pretty disruptive at work wouldn't it?

30 Jun 202614:15

ST: But ppl don't talk about astrology in the workplace!

J: Analogies are not always helpful.

NC: I have finished with that one.

30 Jun 202614:16

NC: You say not asking for special privileges, but you do ask that whenever there are single sex spaces eg ppl should treat you as if you were a woman?

ST: If that is what work policy says then I can, not demanding otherwise.

30 Jun 202614:17

J: I think you acknowledge though that their might be issues, discussion about that?

ST: Yes - as I've said - but not on company time

30 Jun 202614:18

NC: Your WS says here that you think SEEN saying decisions should be on sex not gender, should not be said?

ST: That was DEFRA policy in 2022

30 Jun 202614:18

NC: But surely you can see some women might disagree?

ST: Yes and I've said ppl should be able to express views to line manager for example. Just disagreeing about how and when.

30 Jun 202614:19

NC: But that is exactly what SEEN is trying to do?

ST: Disagree, they are trying to just debate, to delegimise TW in those spaces, on company time.

30 Jun 202614:19

NC: You are saying OK to say it in private but not in public?

ST: They can go and have debates lots of places, it's not OK in the workplace.

30 Jun 202614:20

NC: Are you amember of trades union?

ST: No

ST: Re unions yes

30 Jun 202614:21

NC: But not women?

ST: Have been clear how objections to work policy should be handled.

30 Jun 202614:21

J: Can we take a short break at this point - ten minutes - thank you everyone.

30 Jun 202614:52

ST: That trans ppl in sport should not be mentioned in perpetuity again by the business. p1411.

30 Jun 202614:52

NC: Which part of this do you say is asking that you not express gender ideology beliefs?

ST: [reading ]

30 Jun 202614:55

ST: Sorry - p1417: [reads aloud - para is about withdrawal of several presentations re sport participation, I think]

30 Jun 202614:56

NC: If you read previous paragraph - this is a long email -

ST: yes

NC: It's about changes proposed by John Calvert to a SEEN blog. Starts (p1414) thanks JC for comments and explanations. Complains of unfair scrutiny imposed on SEEN & that other protected beliefs not subject to same.

30 Jun 202614:57

NC: Then goes on to discuss proposed amendments - various colours used for changed, suggestions etc. That is the context for p1417.

ST: Yes

30 Jun 202614:58

NC: Proposed change to a reference to sport participation, notes that there has been much contested discussion. It's true that SEEN is being closely scrutinised by high level managers?

30 Jun 202614:59

30 Jun 202615:00

30 Jun 202615:01

30 Jun 202615:03

30 Jun 202615:03

30 Jun 202615:07

30 Jun 202615:08
