@threadreaderapp please unroll

Wed 01 July AM 1

01 Jul 202607:50

Good morning, its 1 July and the employment tribunal hearing of Samantha Tempest v DEFRA and the Rural Payments Agency will resume this morning, reportedly at 9:30 am.

01 Jul 202607:51

Our coverage to date, plus opening notes from all 3 parties, the witness statement of Samantha Tempest and selected documents can be found on our Substack here.

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

01 Jul 202607:52

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

01 Jul 202607:52

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

01 Jul 202607:52

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

01 Jul 202607:54

Earlier sessions have included discussions of pronouns and how the C is to be referred to. The judge has issued directions. We have copied this from @JRLevinsLaw X post, therefore in the public domain.

01 Jul 202607:55

Abbreviations:

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

01 Jul 202607:55

SEEN/IP – Sex Equality and Equity Network – Civil Service

EW – Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC – Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as Intervenor

J – Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 – Panel member 1

P2 – Panel member 2

AL – Alex Line, barrister for Rs

01 Jul 202607:56

JRL – JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

HH – Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

AB – Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant

AM – Andreas Mueller

GC – gender critical or sex realist views

GI – gender identity

01 Jul 202607:56

TW – transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment TM – transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment

INT – Intervenor

ETBB – Equal Treatment Bench Book

GRC – Gender recognition certificate

PC – Protected characteristic

01 Jul 202607:56

GRA – PC of gender reassignment

DD – Direct discrimination

MLF – My learned friend

CA – Caroline Airs

ED – Emma Dunn

NB – Nicola Bettesworth

VE – Viva Engage

CM – Carly Manning

IAO – Information Asset Owners

NE – Natural England

01 Jul 202607:57

OH – Occupational health

G – Grievance

GA – Grievance appeal

GI – Grievance investigation/investigator

SGI – Second grievance investigation

SGA – Second grievance appeal

01 Jul 202607:57

We will return when the Tribunal is in session.

01 Jul 202608:32

We are WFTCHTJ

(Waiting for the conference host to join). No action yet and conflicting reports on start time.

01 Jul 202608:50

Apologies, it is looking like a start time of 'not before 10 am'.

01 Jul 202609:01

10 am, WFTCHTJ.

Yesterday evening there was discussion of how ST might be able to review the moderation spreadsheet, a request from NC for the Intervenors.

Cross examination of ST by NC is expecting to continue this morning.

01 Jul 202609:16

It's 10:15, we are still WFTCHTJ.

01 Jul 202609:20

We are about to begin.

Admitted to the CVP, some muting and turning off cameras happening.

Video conference abruptly terminated, waiting to rejoin.

01 Jul 202609:23

We are back in the CVP, observers still being admitted.

01 Jul 202609:27

We begin.

NC - we were talking about your claim that SEEN and EW denied the humanity of trans people, a couple of qs, turn to Int bundle, 754, it's a chain of emails, runs backwards, please read it starting with email from EW to page 750, when you've read it let me know.

01 Jul 202609:28

ST reading.

01 Jul 202609:29

J - how many pages do you want us to read?

NC - up to page 750 (email chain, start at bottom, work up)

01 Jul 202609:32

ST - have read

NC - exchange between EW and TIM 'lucy' yes

ST - yes, that's what it says

NC - are you saying that's not what it is

ST - I don't know the people or about the convo

NC - on the face seems warm and

ST - yes, seems friendly

01 Jul 202609:33

J - you are using TIM, Lucy calls self 'transwoman', could you use TW, I know why you're using it, can we use that term, TIM has connotations

NC - I will do as you direct

J - yes,

NC - will do my best, to ST, turn to page ?

ST - I'm there

NC - have you read

ST - yes

01 Jul 202609:35

NC - that's an example, its from Finn Catterel (sp), that what you would call a TM

ST - I don't know it's a neutral name

NC - it's a good example of handling disagreement in the workplace

ST - what disagreement

NC - I may not have quite the right reference

01 Jul 202609:36

NC - turn to page 1270, not sure why it's so far distant from prev ref

ST - yes, got that

NC - you've read it? from Finn Catterel, it's a better example of how to deal with these debates in the workplace

ST - in that respect yes, Finn, is trying to engage, still finding footing

01 Jul 202609:37

finding out what it all means to them, when I commented, there was a point they hadn't considered, in their case they are happy to have a debate, doesn't mean it's suitable for the workplace or suitable or for everyone who's trans

NC - you jump in and say that GC means trans

01 Jul 202609:38

doesn't exist.

ST - you say jump in, I responded to AM's post, he must have expected a reply

J - please slow down

NC, yes, your position is that to not accept you are a woman, is to deny your humanity

ST - I just want respect and consideration in the workplace

01 Jul 202609:40

NC - that means they cannot post any indication that TWAW

ST - it's not about TWAW, its about opening up a discussion about the core of who I am

NC - its right that both men and women are human

ST - yes

NC - you say GC belief refusal to accept TWAW that denies the humanity of

01 Jul 202609:41

transpeople

ST - I've not said exactly that, you're narrowing in on one word and trying to change what I've said

NC - you have said repeatedly that refusing to accept TWAW is denying humanity of TW, it's an unfounded smear, would you like to withdraw it

ST - no

01 Jul 202609:43

NC - you should have a pdf copy of children's book My Body is Me (MBIM).

ST - I don't see it

NC - hands up original copy to ST.

[pause, we cannot see ST but assume looking at MBIM]

ST - yes, I've looked at it

NC - para 7 of your WS says this book and another book

01 Jul 202609:45

discussed by EW are invalidation of your trans identity, is that correct

ST - yes

J - para 7?

NC - checking.....actually it is para 65c. Rather a long subpara, no it's 65e.

J - rather than reading it out, hard to make a note, can you tell us what to look at

01 Jul 202609:47

NC - about 10 lines down, following on from 'for example'

[reading]

NC - you said that you hadn't read either of the books,

ST - I said I had flicked through them

NC - did you read the book I handed you cover to cover

ST - yes

NC - you had not your hands on it previously

01 Jul 202609:49

when you signed your WS. You had no knowledge of the book, you said it was bad because someone told you it was bad and it came from Transgender Trend and you were told it was bad

Wed 01 July PM 1

01 Jul 202612:35

Good afternoon. This is the afternoon session of July 1st, in the claim at employment tribunal by Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency. The morning's session is reported here https://x.com/tribunaltweets/status/2072226291529429038

01 Jul 202612:35

Our substack page about the case is and contains a full list of our abbreviations, and coverage of the earlier days of the hearing.tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-d…

01 Jul 202612:35

The court is currently taking a lunch break, and will restart at 14:05. Naomi Cunningham (NC) will continue cross-examination of claimant Samantha Tempest (ST).

01 Jul 202612:35

NC is the barrister for the case intervenor SEEN (Sex Equality and Equity Network) and its representative Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley (EDW).

01 Jul 202613:10

[Hearing is starting]

01 Jul 202613:11

01 Jul 202613:11

HH: Solicitor will be sending that again - and Emma Dunn's - this evening.

J: Thank you

ST: No. Give me examples.

01 Jul 202613:14

01 Jul 202613:15

J: We have seen examples yes.

01 Jul 202613:16

ST: Can't say - you have to ask HR

01 Jul 202613:20

01 Jul 202613:20

01 Jul 202613:20

ST: I don't agree that I 'roused a mob' as you put it.

01 Jul 202613:21

ST: Agree a lot

NC: Some of them very subtle?

01 Jul 202613:22

01 Jul 202613:23

NC: Not quite my Q - they had to make a lot of judgment calls, some quite subtle

ST: OK Yes

01 Jul 202613:24

01 Jul 202613:24

NC: It's clear they expected pushback?

01 Jul 202613:26

@threadreaderapp please unroll

Thu 02 July AM 1

02 Jul 202608:16

The employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest vs DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency will continue today, 2 July with evidence expected from Emma Dunn. Our coverage of the first morning session will be here.

02 Jul 202608:17

Our previous coverage, plus relevant publicly available documents can be found on our Substack here:

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

02 Jul 202608:18

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

02 Jul 202608:18

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

02 Jul 202608:18

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

02 Jul 202608:20

Earlier sessions have included discussions of pronouns and how the C is to be referred to. The judge has issued directions. We have copied this from @JRLevinsLaw X post, therefore in the public domain.

02 Jul 202608:23

@JRLevinsLaw A personal post by one of the co-chairs of SEEN, has been frequently mentioned. The images below are available from @JRLevinsLaw and on the SEEN crowdfunder, therefore public domain.

02 Jul 202608:24

@JRLevinsLaw Abbreviations:

PQ – Anonymised claimant, no longer a party

ST/C – Samantha Tempest, Claimant

DEFRA/R1 – Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first Respondent

RPA/R2 – Rural Payments Agency, second Respondent

RS – First and second Respondents

02 Jul 202608:24

@JRLevinsLaw SEEN/IP – Sex Equality and Equity Network – Civil Service

EW – Elspeth Duemmer Wrigley, co-chair of SEEN

NC – Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as Intervenor

J – Employment judge SD Robertson

P1 – Panel member 1

P2 – Panel member 2

AL – Alex Line, barrister for Rs

02 Jul 202608:25

@JRLevinsLaw JRL – JR Levins, solicitors for SEEN

HH – Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

AB – Anna Bond, solicitor for claimant

AM – Andreas Mueller

GC – gender critical or sex realist views

GI – gender identity

02 Jul 202608:25

@JRLevinsLaw TW – transwoman, a man claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment TM – transman, woman claiming the protected characteristic of gender assignment

INT – Intervenor

ETBB – Equal Treatment Bench Book

GRC – Gender recognition certificate

PC – Protected characteristic

02 Jul 202608:25

@JRLevinsLaw GRA – PC of gender reassignment

DD – Direct discrimination

MLF – My learned friend

CA – Caroline Airs

ED – Emma Dunn

NB – Nicola Bettesworth

VE – Viva Engage

CM – Carly Manning

IAO – Information Asset Owners

NE – Natural England

02 Jul 202608:26

@JRLevinsLaw OH – Occupational health

G – Grievance

GA – Grievance appeal

GI – Grievance investigation/investigator

SGI – Second grievance investigation

SGA – Second grievance appeal

WFTCHTJ - waiting for the conference host to join (waiting for video conference to begin)

02 Jul 202608:28

The Tribunal is expected to resume at 10 am.

02 Jul 202609:06

We have a view of the empty tribunal room on the video conference. Should be starting soon.

02 Jul 202609:10

The participants and observers are entering the tribunal room.

02 Jul 202609:12

J yes, leading ED through the oath,

ED affirms

HH morning, are you miss, mrs, ms

ED - mrs

HH - can you find your ws in the bundle, should be in lever arch file

ED an awful lot of files and I don't know what any of them are....Int supp bundle...

HH - take your time, it's a smaller bundle,

ED oh they might be lose, 1 of 5, oh it's this one

02 Jul 202609:15

HH I'm sorry, I don't know what page

J second tab

ED I have it

HH go to final page of that section, how many paras

ED. 48

HH is that your signature, have you read it, is it true

ED yes

HH AL will have some qs for you

02 Jul 202609:17

AL you are an employee of the Land Registry (LR)

ED yes HM LR

AL and during the period concerned

ED for 30 years

AL and you are chair of AG (a:gender)

ED co chair,

AL were you sole chair during the period concerned

ED yes

AL AG is xgov network

ED its existence predates your

02 Jul 202609:18

time as chair

AL predates you but Cabinet Office responsible for setting up

ED I don't know

AL in your capacity as chair would you raised issues with CO (Cabinet Office)

ED yes

AL so CO responsible for gov and regulation of AG

ED correct

02 Jul 202609:19

AL you explain in WS function of AG, for T, IS, NB, etc.

ED yes

AL and allies,

ED yes

AL you share a belief that T people should not be discriminated against

ED yes

AL - do you disagree with view that sex is immutable and binary (your personal capacity)

02 Jul 202609:20

ED I do

AL does that represent the view of your members, majority

ED yes, I believe so

AL so do you stand in opposition to SEEN because of GC views

ED a little more complicated, I don't think I stand in opposition, I don't have objections to staff networks, I don't have a

02 Jul 202609:22

personal animosity to anyone for views

AL but you share a view that GC views should not be expressed in the workplace

ED yes

J can I clarify

ED the words 'binary and immutable' is what we find most offensive

AL in that SEEN promotes that, you stand in opposition to that and you

02 Jul 202609:23

have used AG to oppose

ED we stand to protect T colleagues in the workplace

AL because you believe that GC views undermine T people in the workplace, you use AG to undermine SEEN

Thu 02 July PM 1

02 Jul 202612:51

Good afternoon; this is the 2nd July afternoon session in the case of Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency. This morning's evidence can be read at https://x.com/tribunaltweets/status/2072595316180697503

02 Jul 202612:51

Our Substack page on the case includes the previous days' hearings, and a full list of abbreviations

https://tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-defra-and-rural-paymentsâ¦

02 Jul 202612:51

The court is taking its lunch break at present, and we expect the hearing to restart at 2.05pm.

02 Jul 202612:51

Alexander Line (AL) the barrister for the Respondents will complete his cross-examination of Emma Dunn (ED), at the time of events the Chair of a:Gender (aG) - the cross-civil-service staff network for Trans, Non Binary & Intersex staff.

02 Jul 202612:51

ED will then be further cross-examined by Naomi Cunningham (NC), barrister for case intervenors SEEN (Sex Equality & Equity Network), the staff network for gender-critical (GC) civil servants and the SEEN DEFRA departmental network.

[Hearing is about to begin]

02 Jul 202613:17

Fri 03 July AM 1

03 Jul 202608:19

The employment tribunal of Samantha Tempest vs DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency will continue today, 3rd July with continued evidence expected from Emma Dunn. Our coverage of the first morning session will be here.

03 Jul 202608:19

Our previous coverage, plus relevant publicly available documents can be found on our Substack here:

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

03 Jul 202608:19

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

03 Jul 202608:19

Earlier sessions have included discussions of pronouns and how the C is to be referred to. The judge has issued directions. We have copied this from

@JRLevinsLaw

X post, therefore in the public domain.

03 Jul 202608:19

A personal post by one of the co-chairs of SEEN, has been frequently mentioned. The images below are available from

@JRLevinsLaw

and on the SEEN crowdfunder, therefore public domain.

03 Jul 202608:19

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

@threadreaderapp Please unroll

Mon 06 July AM 1

06 Jul 202608:30

Good morning. Today we will be continuing our reporting from the employment tribunal case brought by Samantha Tempest v DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency.

06 Jul 202608:30

This is the start of the third week of this hearing. Our Substack page has our reporting of all the previous days' evidence.

https://tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-defra-and-rural-paymentsâ¦

06 Jul 202608:30

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

06 Jul 202608:30

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs.

Please consider subscribing.

06 Jul 202608:30

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

06 Jul 202608:30

We expect the hearing to begin at 10:00am, and for Steve Moore (at the time of events Director of Major Projects and Programme Delivery Environment at DEFRA) to begin giving evidence.

06 Jul 202608:30

A full list of abbreviations is on our Substack page https://tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-defra-and-rural-paymentsâ¦

The ones likely to be used this morning are:

06 Jul 202608:30

J - Employment Judge S.D. Robertson

P - one or other of the two lay panel members

ST/C - Samantha Tempest, claimant

HH - Helen Hogben KC, barrister for claimant

06 Jul 202608:30

DEFRA/R1 - Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first respondent

RPA/R2 - Rural Payments Agency, second respondent

Rs - First and second respondents

AL - Alexander Line, Respondents' Barrister

06 Jul 202608:30

SEEN - Sex Equality and Equity Network - Civil Service

SEEN-DEFRA - the DEFRA branch of SEEN

NC - Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as intervenor

06 Jul 202608:30

CS: Civil Service

IX: Investigation

Grv: Grievance

(Mr Moore was the decision maker in C's second grievance process)

WS: Witness Statement

06 Jul 202609:01

[Hearing will begin shortly]

06 Jul 202609:06

J: Good morning everyone. We have Mr Moore today.

[SM affirms to tell truth etc]

[AL takes SM through swearing to his WS]

06 Jul 202609:07

HH: [begins cross-examination]

HH: Will be focussing on C second grievance. Your WS para 2 you describe your experience at Environment Agency, many years, v senior, you're now retired?

SM: Yes April 2025

06 Jul 202609:08

HH: You say you'd done several grievances, disciplinaries etc. You worked for EA and at para 7 you say, familiar with EA procedures etc but sought DEFRA HR support for DEFRA ones? Was it re process or re substance of grv?

06 Jul 202609:09

SM: Firstly re grv itself - so I knew if I had time to take it on - and then on policies etc. Which I found similar / identical.

06 Jul 202609:10

HH: You say broadly similar, do you agree that there are common ingredients in *all* orgs to a fair grv?

SM: Yes

HH: Transparency etc?

SM: Yes

06 Jul 202609:11

HH: Investigate facts, decide any action, inform employee?

SM: yes

HH: Keeping accurate records?

SM: of key elements

HH: And no unreasonable delay?

SM: Yes, but with caveat of doing a fair job.

06 Jul 202609:11

HH: There are additional ingredients but main issue was failure to implement Janet Hughes' recommendations?

SM: Yes

06 Jul 202609:12

HH: Before this grv -

J: I am not sure SM's prior experience will help us much. We are tight on time.

HH: I will short cut. Did you have any experience before on complaints re Gender Reassignment harassment?

06 Jul 202609:13

SM: General bullying and harassment cases, and a few - not identical but similar to this. And had specialist advice / training here (which is legal privileged).

06 Jul 202609:14

HH: Did that previous grievance concern SEEN?

SM: To an extent.

HH: Complaint about their existence?

SM: I can't remember whether that was specifically part of it.

06 Jul 202609:15

HH: Allegations of discriminatory posts by SEEN / members?

SM: Can't recall that level of detail?

HH: Re postings on Yammer etc?

SM: About conflicting views being expressed on open platform.

06 Jul 202609:16

J: Previous case similar how?

SM: trans member of staff, issues of data protection and use of Yammer, but I don't have it all in front of me and can't recall all details.

HH: before ST grv?

SM: yes

HH: How long?

SM: poss spring 2023? Wd need to check

06 Jul 202609:16

HH: Outcome?

SM: upheld that one.

06 Jul 202609:17

HH: So, related issues data protection & use of Yammer - was previous one about posting GC beliefs?

SM: It was more general than that I think - whether any member of staff could post views on open platform and should that be the way we worked.

06 Jul 202609:18

SM: I think there were plans to improve the situation but I think I upheld the grv because those were not being implemented effectively

06 Jul 202609:19

HH: So you have broad understanding of issues. For ST - issue of dealing with grvs without unreasonable delay. ST submitted 19/6. You weren't appointed until end July?

06 Jul 202609:19

SM: I wasn't appointed then, was approached - I think it had been mentioned to me early, but formal contact by DEFRA HR then to see if I could take it on. 28th July I think

06 Jul 202609:20

HH: And not finalised for another month or so?

SM: I had to work out whether I had time to take it on, I think DEFRA HR jumped gun and told ST I had been appointed late July, but I was then on leave in August so I asked that ST be informed I would start September after leave.

06 Jul 202609:21

HH: Most grievances are done and dusted in that time

SM: Far from it - complex cases take sometimes months. Esp if at director level - ppl don't have much spare time. They genuinely take a long time.

06 Jul 202609:22

HH: So want to discuss how long yr appointment took. There are 20,000 odd employees - it took much too long to appoint investigator.

J: What period?

HH: six weeks about

06 Jul 202609:23

HH: No attempt to find alternative investigator?

SM: I wouldn't know. DEFRA HR.

HH: Who appointed you?

SM: Wd have to look at emails - was it Jackie?

06 Jul 202609:23

HH: You had a chat with her 28/7?

SM: Yes

06 Jul 202609:25

HH: p1088-9

HH: email to you from Jackie - appears you had a meeting with her that day?

SM: a discussion

HH: last para - says "meeting"?

SM: We will have had one, for me to evaluate scale of poss involvement, time etc

06 Jul 202609:26

HH: At this point you had Janet Hughes' grv outcome?

SM: Don't remember

HH: You took no notes of the meeting?

SM: At this stage it's speculative, do I have capacity, may have scribbled some notes but not involved yet, hadn't agreed to do it.

06 Jul 202609:27

HH: This is a complex case.

SM: As I found out

HH: And we don't have any notes of your decision making process?

SM: I don't have copies - still at EA maybe? I don't know.

HH: Did you make notes?

SM: I tend to yes

HH: Did you?

SM: Can't remember I'm afraid

06 Jul 202609:27

HH: Re transparency - even more important in such a case as this?

SM: I don't think so re an initial conversation with HR, no, Other stages yes - mtgs with C, outcome

06 Jul 202609:28

HH: We have typed copies, not your handwritten ones?

SM: yes

06 Jul 202609:29

HH: p[missed]

HH: This is the resolution policy you used? In force at the time?

SM: Yes

HH: p2012 - policy is to help ppl carry out duties with dignity?

SM: Yes

06 Jul 202609:30

HH: And C not able to do that, bcs SEEN posting content that denied existence

SM: Yes understood that

06 Jul 202609:31

HH: p2015-6 - policy section re BHD (bullying harassment discrimination) para 16 - reinforces need for speed?

SM: That's 17? [discussion of which policy para is which]

06 Jul 202609:32

HH: BHD needs swift action?

SM: And effective

HH: And particularly necessary to prevent BHD continuing?

SM: I think aim is to do as quickly and fairly and effectively as poss

06 Jul 202609:32

HH: In this case, C is saying, harassment is continuing, already done 1 grv?

SM: Yes

06 Jul 202609:33

HH: You say you took some specific advice from HR - same convo as we've discussed or another time?

SM: Can't recall - I think diff, re policies.

06 Jul 202609:35

HH: p1448 this is 9/8/23 Chris Brown (HR) contacts you, you ask to discuss and then did on 10/8. CB role was to coordinate all CS requests?

SM: I don't know

06 Jul 202609:35

HH: Had you seen grv, and the previous one, at this point?

SM: May have - probably had - I think yes had been sent some docs yes

06 Jul 202609:36

HH: Did you discuss grv itself with CB? Its content?

SM: I think it was more about process, esp the IX manager - to get an investigation sorted?

HH: You'd already discussed that with Jackie.

SM: No, that had been about my availablity etc

06 Jul 202609:37

HH: Did you talk to CB about the content? On 9-10/8?

SM: Can't remember

HH: No notes of that mtg?

SM: No

06 Jul 202609:38

HH: Back to the dispute resolution policy - paras 71-72 clear that all stages must have written record. That's 2 things you didn't take notes?

SM: I would argue that process didn't start until I met with ST to agree scope.

06 Jul 202609:38

J: Any notes at all?

SM: Yes I would have written notes in a notebook

HH: Did you give those rough notes to HR?

SM: no

06 Jul 202609:39

HH: You didn't contact ST until 8/9/23 and you met the next week.

HH: p1553 [I think]

HH: This is your email to ST 29/9 attaching terms of reference?

SM: yes

06 Jul 202609:41

HH: Grv 19/6, terms not finalised until 3 months later?

SM: yes

HH: p2021, the policy. Para37 says that decision maker shd actively monitor, to have things as quick as poss. If not sorted in 30 days, review inc by HR. You didn't review?

SM: Not formally no.

06 Jul 202609:41

SM: Was v aware of the time taken, and that in September we were looking for IX manager

06 Jul 202609:42

J: Do you know if there was a progress review? By you or HR?

SM: We were constantly reviewing - I would say weekly?

J: Under terms of para 37?

SM: Not in the formal sense no

06 Jul 202609:43

HH: Is it fair to say that at end Sept, DEFRA had had a number of complaints from trans staff inc the one you had dealt with in the spring?

SM: That was not DEFRA.

06 Jul 202609:44

HH: You were aware of a complaint similar to ST's. Aware of probs with use of Yammer.

SM: I don't really use it but yes aware.

HH: Aware of lots of complaints re GC content?

SM: I don't know. Was not put in front of me, no.

06 Jul 202609:45

and I asked for additional examples and there were none.

Tue 07 July AM 1

07 Jul 202608:40

The employment tribunal of Tempest v DEFRA and Rural Payments Agency continues today, 7 July. Expected start time, not before 10 am.

07 Jul 202608:41

Our previous reporting, background information on the case, witness statements (after witnesses are sworn), opening notes from counsel and other relevant documents can be found on our Substack.

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

07 Jul 202608:42

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

07 Jul 202608:42

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs. Please consider subscribing to our Substack, link in bio.

07 Jul 202608:42

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

07 Jul 202608:45

We expect that the cross examination of Nicola Bettesworth, DEFRA Chief People Officer by Helen Hogben, to resume this morning.

Abbreviations that may be used are below.

07 Jul 202608:46

J - Employment Judge S.D. Robertson

P - one or other of the two lay panel members

ST/C - Samantha Tempest, claimant

HH - Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

DEFRA/R1 - Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first respondent

07 Jul 202608:47

RPA/R2 - Rural Payments Agency, second respondent

Rs - First and second respondents

AL - Alexander Line, Respondents' Barrister

SEEN - Sex Equality and Equity Network - Civil Service SEEN-DEFRA - the DEFRA branch of SEEN

07 Jul 202608:48

NC - Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as intervenor

CS: Civil Service

IX: Investigation

Grv: Grievance

WS: Witness Statement

07 Jul 202608:48

We will return when the hearing gets under way.

07 Jul 202609:03

WFTCHTJ

07 Jul 202609:06

Looks like we will begin soon. Participants and observers are being admitted to the hearing room.

07 Jul 202609:09

J morning everyone, we are continuing with NB's evidence

HH good morning NB, just let me find the page I need. We were talking y'day about post on page 125, and look at the WS of Carolyn Airs (CA).

J - it's not at the front of the witness bundle, what para

HH - para 25, CA

07 Jul 202609:11

sets out procedure for moderation before Dec 2022 (?) and before PoC (principles of communication) moderation done by DDTS (DEFRA IT). Moderation was reactive,

NB yes

HH no PoC in place at that time

NB correct we relied on our code of conduct and expectation of professional

07 Jul 202609:12

behaviour

HH the decision had been made to allow SEEN in DEFRA and for them to be on Yammer, a decision had been made that the expression of GC views on Yammer would be permitted

NB we recognised that people would use that platform to express a range of views and that would

07 Jul 202609:14

GC views. These expressed as individual views.

HH you took no steps to intervene in this decision to allow GC views on Yammer

NB Yammer widely used, and when I received CAs briefing I saw no basis on which to proactively limit use of Yammer (Y)

HH no basis to proactively

07 Jul 202609:15

limit the use of Y, but also no steps to consider how expression of views would impact trans colleague

NB I don't agree, as CA note sets out in next steps we were alive to the heightened risk of difficult communications and were looking to create the ability for people to engage

07 Jul 202609:16

in respectful professional way.

HH looking at 126, 127, at the time that decision was made to allow open expression, there had been no engagement with trans colleagues or those who represent trans colleagues

NB not to my knowledge, and can I clarify point on permission to use

07 Jul 202609:17

Y, it had been available views to all employees, I was conferring permission to express GC views, people had the right to express a range of views with the guardrails of the code of conduct

HH talking about permission, when inappropriate content goes on Y, DEFRA HR had a respon

07 Jul 202609:19

to step in.

NB DEFRA had a responsibility to step in if CoC was breached, not exclusively with HR, digital colleagues involved,

CoC - code of conduct

HH para 20 of WS, you say that it was your understanding that assessing suitability of SEEN for CS and necessity for any

07 Jul 202609:20

equality impact assessment would have been undertaken by Cabinet Office.

NB yes for cross dept networks

HH you took no steps to assure yourself that had happened

NB correct, my team in regular contact with Cabinet Office team, they would have known steps followed for recognition

07 Jul 202609:21

of impact

HH if you knew that equality impact assessment had taken place you would have said so in your WS

NB I did not consider for my WS, I cannot say it does or does not exist

HH even if carried out, doesn't absolve DEFRA of considering impact on own org,

NB correct

07 Jul 202609:22

HH because PSED is not something that can be delegated

NB correct

HH para 21, talk about formation of DEFRA staff networks, if based around EA protected belief, eq impact assessment not standardly done, there's not specific policy to support that is there

NB (no response heard)

07 Jul 202609:24

HH that is an after the event justification that you did not carry out a formal assessment of the impact of the network

NB I disagree with that, despite cross gov network, we considered carefully the impact at DEFRA, you can see that in CA's briefing note. Its not correct to say

07 Jul 202609:25

that we came into this without any consideration.

J not exactly what was put to - repeats HH q, no policy, what do you say in response to that assertion

NB that is not what I was intending to say, we took lots of steps, did we have a formal assessment, I don't know.

J WS says

07 Jul 202609:27

'we would not undertake an assessment', is there a policy that says that

NB I'm not aware of any policy

J why did you say 'we would not undertake'

NB there may be a sort of practice that is based on discussions with CO team, I don't believe there is a formal policy that says we

07 Jul 202609:28

will or won't.

HH isn't this the type of post match analysis that ECHR warns against,

AL I'm a bit lost as to the impact of these things

J I asked a specific question about the WS, HH you are going beyond that

HH this the type of post match analysis that ECHR warns against,

07 Jul 202609:29

I'm talking about the post match justification to allow DEFRA SEEN as a network.

AL I'm in your hands, the question has been asked,

HH I suspect I'll get the same answer, so I'll move on. Due regard to PSED means conscious approach to further aims and objectives

NB I agree

07 Jul 202609:30

HH must be done with integrity and rigour, is a continuing duty,

NB I agree

HH important when exercising public functions, says whether to allow DEFRA SEEN as a network, to keep records to show you actively considered impact

NB I agree

07 Jul 202609:32

HH you did none of those things did you

NB I don't agree with that. Against a backdrop that SEEN had already been rec'd as x departmental network, we gave thought to how to manage, we saw it is a challenging issue in DEFRA we did not move into with consideration and we knew we

07 Jul 202609:33

would have to continue a careful focus on it.

HH agree challenging sensitive issue for DEFRA because it was going impact rights of trans colleagues and their right to come to work and feel safe

NB a sensitive issue, we had to find a very careful path to balance needs and wants

07 Jul 202609:34

J we are talking about using the word issue, when consideration was being given to recognition of DEFRA SEEN, what was the issue

NB the issue was the sort of polarised and very different perspective between elements of the work force, I was aware of from a xgov perspective,

07 Jul 202609:35

following CA briefing became aware of heightened tensions in DEFRA.

HH your answer there - describing the issue as polarised perspectives in the workplace, you were looking at this as competing beliefs, not beliefs vs lived experience of other

07 Jul 202609:36

NB No we were looking at this from the perspective of the law, as an employer, where people could feel safe and where CoC and Nolan Principles (NP) were embodied in the work place. It wasn't 2 dimensional, we were thinking about if from a broad perspective

07 Jul 202609:37

HH so the more serious the equality impact, the more serious the consideration should be,

NB I agree

HH for trans colleagues, this was questioning their existence, required an evidence based analysis of impact

NB yes

HH you didn't do that, did you

07 Jul 202609:38

NB there was lots of careful consideration, engagement with Cab Office, we took advice, this was a difficult pathway we were trying to work through, in our minds was the experiences and needs of all of our work force. So absolutely we thought about the impact on TG colleagues.

07 Jul 202609:40

But we had needs and wants from other parts of our workforce that we had to consider equally.

HH 2 final qs on this point, even w/o a formal assessment we don't seen any alternative consideration of evidence based assessment

NB CA's briefing note can show you the work that was

07 Jul 202609:41

done, we gave it real consideration, we didn't just step into it.

HH what is clear from evidence is that as opposed to stepping back before decision taken, you adopted position proposed by CA in her note of July 22, and did not waver

J what do you say that position was HH

07 Jul 202609:42

HH open expression of GC beliefs on open platform

HH repeats 'you adopted CA position of allowing expression of GC beliefs on open platform, you adopted that position and rallied around it'.

NB rallied around is not a good description, Y was already available, the EA protects

07 Jul 202609:43

beliefs and expression and of beliefs, it wasn't about myself or CA allowing expression of beliefs. What we didn't do was adopt a punitive position saying that we were going to restrict expression of beliefs on Y or any other platform.

07 Jul 202609:44

HH have adopted a position, we say, all your actions were reactive rather than anticipatory

NB we don't take and simply hold on to positions, the way I and my teams work, we are constantly reviewing and assessing evidence to determine a way forward and thats what we did here.l

07 Jul 202609:46

HH lets talk about formation of xgov SEEN and what happened in DEFRA after that, post from EDW Dec 2022, announcing SEEN xgov. Immediately provoked responses in support of trans colleauges.

NB I don't recall, but I suspect so.

HH C posted that day, numerous comments from

07 Jul 202609:47

lots and lots of colleagues, you're aware of those comments

NB yes

J did you anticipate those comments

NB we expected a reaction but we expected that employees would behave in accordance with CoC and EDI policies,

J one more q, did you know in advance that EW was going to post

07 Jul 202609:48

this in advance

NB [pause] I'm not certain

J if your team had known, would you have known

NB not necessarily

HH given the response that we see, you were on notice that the formation of dept SEEN was likely to have adverse effect on employees who were trans

07 Jul 202609:50

NB reasonable to say that addition of dept network on top of x-gov network would not be welcomed by trans colleagues

HH not just not welcomed, it was causing harm, whether you agree with justified it was causing harm and distress

NB telling me they were distressed and discontent

07 Jul 202609:51

and went to the core of their being

HH and yet knowing those views, at that point you undertook no risk assessment of dept SEEN network

NB I'm not certain that we filled out a formal risk assessment but were we alive to the risks and issues - very much so. Very similar to the

07 Jul 202609:52

issues once the xgov SEEN was established.

HH at that point, bearing in mind continuing duty, you didn't do a equality impact assessment, you didn't take stock then

NB I don't know the detail of the work done in my team, it is correct to say that we were keeping the position

07 Jul 202609:53

under constant review. In looking at the issue our job in HR is to consider all elements of the law and to ensure that we are sort of applying all dimensions of the law and not be one dimensional.

HH you say under constant review, but none in evidence vs the PSED

07 Jul 202609:54

NB I use the word review to cover thinking about it, considering it, looking at our policies, it was part of our enduring day to day activity, there was a period where this sort of matter of gender critical/gender identity was consuming the HR EDI team. We did not consider in

07 Jul 202609:56

a light touch way, it was very absorbing.

J how was this working out, was there working parties was there correspondence, documents, reports or assessments?

NB the EDI team is 6 people, this was there day to day activity, conversations, emails between them, convos with inclusive

07 Jul 202609:57

practice teams, convos with other colleagues in other orgs part of the same EDI community

HH the decision to permit DEFRA SEEN was based on the view that refusal would be less favourable treatment with those of GC beliefs

NB yes, and against a backdrop that DEFRA had a culture

07 Jul 202609:58

of networks and community groups and we valued those networks

HH the decision to permit them to be established was because it would be discriminatory not to, no consideration if discrim against those the GRA PC.

NB we considered and weighed up all those elements, we seek to

07 Jul 202609:59

operate as lawful body, particularly important when you are a gov body. That consideration was how we approached the recognition of DEFRA SEEN. Against a backdrop that SEEN had be recognised as xdept network.

J put to you more specifically, did you consider whether GC views to

07 Jul 202610:00

expressed via DEFRA SEEN would be discrim to trans colleagues,

NB we considered

J any documentary evidence of that decison

NB don't know

J if you did consider, what the result of that consideration, discriminatory against trans colleagues or not

07 Jul 202610:01

NB my team would have recognised the impact on trans colleagues and allies but on balance we concluded that our responsibility to comply with EA to allow people to express beliefs was an important dimension, we didn't believe that existence of SEEN and GC views was necessarily

07 Jul 202610:03

discrimination, the risk was the way in which it would be manifested. The mitigation was that we expected our colleagues to behave in a professional manner, free from bullying harassment or discrimination.

HH follow up on judge's question - did you form a view that it was

07 Jul 202610:04

discriminatory, you were more concerned and exclusively that you would protect yourself against a claim of discrim on philosophical belief and did not concern yourself with the trans people in the org

NB I do not agree with that

HH a couple of qs about continuing nature of PSED

07 Jul 202610:05

NB at time org was making decision to allow DEFRA SEEN, you'll agree there was evidence of distress to trans colleagues

J when are we talking about

HH announcement of x dept SEEN in Nov 22,

NB yes

HH so across that period you were receiving numerous grievances from trans

07 Jul 202610:07

colleagues

NB I would not say numerous,

HH how many were you aware of

NB I would say I was aware of 2 more beyond STs, others may have been dealt with at dept level

HH you had JH letter in response to 1st g/x from ST, you accept completely that she was distressed and very upset

07 Jul 202610:08

NB yes

HH you had all of that evidence but at no stage did you take conscious steps to consider that evidence against PSED

NB I was very alive to the discourse and exchanges happening across DEFRA, we will never know but the fact that DEFRA SEEN was rec'd was already a x dept

07 Jul 202610:09

SEEN network, the EA allowed the expression of GC beliefs our employees in DEFRA already had the ability to express, engage and share their views at an individual level. Regardless if we had recognised SEEN at dept level, we continue to vigilant about the way that people

07 Jul 202610:11

conducted themselves within the CoC.

HH my question was that you took no steps to evaluate that evidence

J you have referred repeatedly to PSED, we have no jurisdiction over that, why are you referring to it repeatedly

HH its for the indirect discrimination claim -

07 Jul 202610:12

proportionate means to a legitimate end. Repeats qs. NB I disagree, we kept it under constant review, we monitored to try and create the right environment for our employees

HH you mentioned that employees already had opportunity to express GC beliefs individually or through xdept

07 Jul 202610:13

SEEN, was that not sufficient

NB we had a request for recognition, against the backdrop of the EA and the culture of DEFRA, we had already recognised subgroups of faith and belief networks; Christian, Jewish, Muslim. We were seeking to ensure that we were acting to comply with

07 Jul 202610:14

EA and then attempting to risk manage the issues that it might present. We were trying to do that in a careful way, I genuinely believe we were not an employer that was observing a problem we were trying to manage a difficult set of issues in a careful considered way.

07 Jul 202610:16

HH given the evidence that you had of impact and distress the fact that SEEN member already had ability to express was an important in the balancing act

J you're being asked if you needed another one

NB I don't know that need came into it, we had a number of networks at dept

07 Jul 202610:17

level that also existed at a xdept level. The question for me and my team was did we want to treat SEEN differently.

HH and that was ultimately the prevailing factor in your mind - fear of a claim of direct discrim

NB fear of a claim was not the prime reason, we sought to create

07 Jul 202610:19

a working environment that was fair, where people felt valued, it wasn't a sense of fear of a claim against us

HH email from CA to TW, copying Sarah McNally, you're not copied on this email, can I ask you about page 141, CA is saying this is in the context of re-issuing post

07 Jul 202610:20

from EW, says that threat of discrim claim was not an idle threat, and tribunals seem sympathetic to GC views, were you aware of this

NB I was not copied on this, even if she had said to me her view about a claim and an employment tribunal, it's not an exceptional occurrence for

07 Jul 202610:21

employees to signal that they might or could go to an employment tribunal. I come back to operating with integrity, doing the right thing, doing the lawful thing would always be my focus, not whether someone could take us to an employment tribunal.

HH this was not just an

07 Jul 202610:22

employee, a senior lawyer in Natural England

J witness has already said she didn't know

HH doc says CA has already alerted you to potential for claim or tribunal, you say she didn't expand on the threat

NB you were asking me about the view of NE, CA had alerted me to an EAT

07 Jul 202610:23

on that topic and what it meant.

HH moving on to a new topic....

J let's have a short break, so we will have 15 minutes, back at 11:40.

Morning session part one ends.

@threadreaderapp unroll please

Tue 07 July PM 1

07 Jul 202612:45

Good afternoon and welcome to the afternoon session on 7th July of the employment tribunal of Tempest v DEFRA and Rural Payments Agency.

Expected 2.05pm start.

07 Jul 202612:45

For full coverage of this case, including extensive abbreviations, case details and documents, please visit our substack.

https://tribunaltweets.substack.com/p/tempest-vs-defra-and-rural-paymentsâ¦

07 Jul 202612:45

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

07 Jul 202612:45

We expect the cross examination of Nicola Bettesworth (NB), DEFRA Chief People Officer by Helen Hogben, to continue this afternoon.

Reminder: Our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith

07 Jul 202612:45

Abbreviations

J - Employment Judge S.D. Robertson

P - 1 or other of 2 lay panel members

ST/C - Samantha Tempest, claimant

HH - Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

DEFRA/R1 - Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, 1st respondent

07 Jul 202612:45

RPA/R2 - Rural Payments Agency, second respondent

Rs - First and second respondents

AL - Alexander Line, Respondents' Barrister

SEEN - Sex Equality and Equity Network - Civil Service SEEN-DEFRA - the DEFRA branch of SEEN

NC - Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as intervenor

07 Jul 202612:45

CS: Civil Service

IX: Investigation

Grv/gx: Grievance

WS: Witness Statement

CoC - Code of Conduct

GC - Gender Critical

GR - Gender Reassignment

PC - Protected Characteristic

HR - Human Resources

EDI - Equality Diversity & Inclusion

07 Jul 202613:10

[Court resumes 14.09]

HH - Afternoon again NB. Before we move onto 1st gx appeal I want to go through some things we discussed before lunch. Go to 551 of bundle. Ed Parry was decision maker on gx. We can see his decision letter to C. Go to page 553.

@threadreaderapp please unroll

Wed 08 July AM 1

08 Jul 202608:28

The employment tribunal of Tempest v DEFRA & RPA continues this morning, 8 July. Start time, expected not before 10 am. This is the first morning session.

08 Jul 202608:28

Our previous reporting, background information on the case, witness statements (after witnesses are sworn), opening notes from counsel and other relevant documents can be found on our Substack.

open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltw…

Tempest vs DEFRA & Rural Payments Agency Tempest is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. https://open.substack.com/pub/tribunaltweets/p/tempest-vs-defra-and-rural-payments?r=1gxdhb&utm_campaign=post-expanded-share&utm_medium=web

08 Jul 202608:29

Tempest (the claimant or C) is claiming discrimination, harassment and/or victimisation on grounds of gender reassignment. Central to the claim is the Sex Equality and Equity Network in the Civil Service (SEEN or IP). SEEN has been granted right to intervene.

08 Jul 202608:29

We are a small group of volunteer citizen journalists. We receive no payment for our work. We accept paid subscriptions to our Substack to fund necessary expenses including travel, legal fees and digital costs. Please consider subscribing to our Substack, link in bio.

08 Jul 202608:29

A reminder that our work is not a verbatim account or transcript of proceedings. We make our best efforts to report what is said and do so in good faith.

08 Jul 202608:31

We expect that the cross examination of Nicola Bettesworth, DEFRA Chief People Officer by Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN, to conclude this morning. Carolyn Airs, is expected to be the next witness.

Abbreviations that may be used are below

08 Jul 202608:31

J - Employment Judge S.D. Robertson

P - one or other of the two lay panel members

ST/C - Samantha Tempest, claimant

HH - Helen Hogben, barrister for claimant

DEFRA/R1 - Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, first respondent

08 Jul 202608:32

RPA/R2 - Rural Payments Agency, second respondent

Rs - First and second respondents

AL - Alexander Line, Respondents' Barrister

SEEN - Sex Equality and Equity Network - Civil Service SEEN-DEFRA - the DEFRA branch of SEEN

08 Jul 202608:32

NC - Naomi Cunningham, barrister for SEEN as intervenor

CS: Civil Service

IX: Investigation

Grv: Grievance

WS: Witness Statement

08 Jul 202608:35

CA - Carolyn Airs, at the relevant time was Inclusive Practice and Senior Policy Advisor at DEFRA, in EDI team. Has since retired.

We will return when the hearing begins.

08 Jul 202608:38

Possibly of interest to our followers; @SexMattersOrg is taking the CPS to court over its guidance on deception and consent. @nickwallis may be reporting.

08 Jul 202608:57

We are WFTCHTJ

08 Jul 202609:13

It is 10:10, we have not been admitted to the video conference. Experience to date is that clerks have started video conference promptly; likely that the start time is delayed, we have no information on why.

08 Jul 202609:17

We have been admitted to the CVP. We should begin shortly.

08 Jul 202609:22

Participants have entered.
